programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A teenage girl goes missing while out partying with friends, and a murder investigation is launched when she is found sexually assaulted and strangled in a local park. David Caruso stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.desc: Sharon develops a disturbing aversion to work, while Dorien is disgusted by Tracey's amazing ability to attract men, a skill seemingly unhindered by her pregnancy. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A gang member is shot and his dying words in the ambulance are the name of corrupt ex-policeman Ray Moore. programme.desc: Faye realises she must tell Joseph the truth when he walks out of theatre unable to complete an operation. programme.title: Casualty

programme.title: The Bill

Drama, guest starring Eric Sykes, Lindsey Coulson and Victor McGuire

Meanwhile, Harry meets Elena at the ballet, but Russian counter-intelligence agents are on her trail, forcing her son, Sasha, to take action to prevent his mother being revealed as a former western spy. Peter Firth, Alice Krige and Geoffrey Streatfeild star

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Glanaethwy yn 30
programme.desc: A concert to celebrate the school choir's 30th birthday from Venue Cymru in Llandudno, with founder Cefin Roberts leading the singers and their guests through interpretations of popular works, pieces by some of Wales' most prominent composers, and contributions from notable soloists

programme.title: Heno
programme.desc: An entertaining mix bidding farewell to the strange year of 2020. There's a special song by Geraint Lovgreen, one of Wrexham's biggest fans, to celebrate the purchase of the club by two of Hollywood's biggest stars. The Cabarela crew have two new songs looking back over the year, and we enjoy an emotional song from some fabulous Welsh West End stars, led by Steffan Rhys Hughes. Plus, a special interview with world-renowned star Matthew Rhys and contributions from Rhys Meirion, Aled Hall and Trystan Llyr Griffiths

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Edne's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Mr Stink (2012)
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville stars, with Sheridan Smith, Johnny Vegas and Isabella Blake-Thomas, and an appearance from Britain's Got Talent winner Pudsey the dog

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Frozen (2013)
programme.desc: A princess with the power to freeze anything she touches loses control of her abilities and plunges the kingdom into a perpetual winter. She goes into hiding, but her sister remains determined that she can be saved from her own magic and sets off on a quest to find her, aided by a mountain man and a living snowman. Disney animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kristen Bell and Idina Menzel

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present another edition of their unorthodox, star-filled quiz show. This time, all the contestants are from the world of music, and as befits the time of year, the entry requirement is that they must have released a successful Christmas hit. Stop the Cavalry singer Jona Lewie teams up with Mike Batt, who released Wombling Merry Christmas, while 1972 Christmas Number One hit-maker Jimmy Osmond pairs with his 2015 counterpart - X Factor's Ben Haenow. Choirmaster Gareth Malone joins forces with Samantha Stevenson of the Military Wives Choir, and Justin and Dan Hawkins of The Darkness also compete, before playing out the show with their festive favourite Christmas Time (Don't Let the Bells End)

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick is desperate to help Denise and takes matters into his own hands - although he may have only made matters worse. Ruby warns Stacey to stay out of her marriage, while Jay makes out that the kiss with Honey was a mistake - is he hiding his true feelings?

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first of two special Christmas episodes of the culinary challenge, in which eight finalists and semi-finalists from past years return to face a festive test of their skills. The first episode features Danny Parker from the 2014 series, 2017's Louisa Ellis, 2009's Marianne Lumb, and 2018 semi-finalist William Chilila. Monica Galetti sets them the task of making festive canapés, before they move on to the dreaded skills test and a seasonally themed two-course menu

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Adela and Father Roberts prove unlikely allies for Ruth, forcing Clodagh to impose strict order or risk losing control of the convent, as well as herself. But events escalate as Dilip Rai and Kanchi are discovered together, with dramatic consequences. Ruth flees the palace to declare her love to Mr Dean, who is defending the convent against retaliation from the village. Conclusion of the adaptation of Rumer Godden's novel, starring Gemma Arterton and Alessandro Nivola

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford and Brighton & Hove Albion v Arsenal at Amex Stadium. United and Wolves met on four occasions in all competitions last term, with three of those contests ending level and the Red Devils prevailing 1-0 at home in an FA Cup replay in the other. Brighton did the double over the Gunners last season, winning 2-1 home and away, and will be hopeful of shocking the Londoners once again. Plus, a round-up of the rest of tonight and yesterday's fixtures

programme.title: Point Break (1991)
programme.desc: An FBI agent infiltrates a Californian surfing gang suspected of carrying out a series of armed robberies wearing masks modelled on former US presidents. However, his loyalties are tested to the limits when he is befriended by the gang's leader. Kathryn Bigelow's thriller, starring Keanu Reeves, Patrick Swayze, Gary Busey and Lori Petty

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The annual harvest festival brings the whole village together, but one resident's announcement causes unexpected emotional consequences for the community. Spoof documentary exploring modern life in the Cotswolds, written by and starring siblings Daisy May and Charlie Cooper

programme.title: Anita & Me (2002)
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Meena strikes up an unlikely friendship with blonde teenage tearaway Anita. Despite their ethnic and social differences, the girls manage to support each other through difficult times while their families cope with life's problems. Comedy drama set in a Midlands village in the 1970s and based on the novel by Meera Syal, starring Chandeep Uppal, Anna Brewster, Max Beesley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kathy Burke, Lynn Redgrave and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Strange Magic (2015)
programme.desc: A fairy living in an enchanted forest tries to rescue her sister from a monstrous creature. Meanwhile, the magical kingdom is thrown into chaos by a love potion that results in a series of unexpected romances. Animated fantasy musical, with the voices of Evan Rachel Wood, Alan Cumming and Kristin Chenoweth

programme.title: Early Man (2018)
programme.desc: A caveman tribe must learn to play football in order to regain their homeland from a conquering civilisation. Animation, with the voice of Eddie Redmayne

programme.title: Gangsta Granny
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Schoolboy Ben is bored witless after being made to stay with his grandma. All she ever wants to do is play Scrabble and eat yucky cabbage soup. But it turns out there's a whole lot more to this OAP than cardigans and toffees when she tells him how she used to be an international jewel thief - and when she admits there was one heist she never quite managed in her heyday, the pair set out on a criminal adventure. Julia McKenzie stars as the shady senior citizen, with Walliams and Miranda Hart as the boys' ballroom-dancing parents, alongside Joanna Lumley, Rob Brydon and Robbie Williams, with Reece Buttery as Ben

programme.title: Room on the Broom
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It tells the story of a kind witch who invites a surprising number of animals to ride on her broom - much to the frustration of her cat. But when a fearsome dragon appears, what will the gang do? Gillian Anderson heads the voice cast as the witch, with Rob Brydon, Martin Clunes, Sally Hawkins and David Walliams as the animals, Timothy Spall as the dragon and Simon Pegg as narrator

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake

programme.title: Dragons: Gift of the Night Fury
programme.desc: Animated short film featuring the characters from How to Train Your Dragon, in which the Vikings prepare to celebrate their winter festival, only to find all the winged reptiles have mysteriously disappeared. Hiccup tries to discover where they have gone, and stumbles on a mysterious island. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Young Viking Hiccup has successfully persuaded his people to live in peace with dragons, but now faces a new threat from a warlord who is capturing the flying lizards to build an army. As Hiccup tries to keep the peace, he is reunited with his long-lost mother. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Jay Baruchel, Gerard Butler and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host another sport-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Famous faces Scarlett Moffatt, Matt Everitt, Lucrezia Millarini and Stephen K Amos are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of The Sitcom Bottom, Glastonbury Festival, Die Hard and Fela Kuti

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Among the items coming into the barn for a spruce-up include an iconic but dilapidated Chopper bike which once lit up Christmas morning for two brothers. A much-loved but threadbare teddy bear in need of a stitch in time provides another challenge, along with a broken-down clockwork train set and a treasured harmonium which has been silent for decades. Jay Blades presents

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four of the most memorable professional chefs from past series - Steve Barringer, Exose Grant Lopo-Ndigna, Oli Martin and Olivia Burt - are invited to return to the MasterChef kitchen to compete in a festive-themed special. First, the chefs have the chance to relive the much-feared Skills Test, and catch up with judges on how their careers have progressed since taking part in the competition. Later, they are tasked to create a seasonally themed two-course menu in two-and-half hours to impress the judges

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy plan some New Year's Eve festivities in this Christmas special, with both inviting their parents to join them. Things soon go badly wrong as party games give way to an uncompromising round of guests pointing out what New Year's resolutions everyone needs to make - with a distinct lack of festive spirit on display. Starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Pandemonium
programme.desc: One-off video diary comedy, in which a boy documents his family's disastrous year. With their summer holiday to California having been cancelled and their finances devastated due to the pandemic, family morale is at an all-time low. But the Jessops make one final stab at trying to salvage something from 2020 with a trip to Margate in the middle of December

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Newcastle United v Liverpool at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Fulham at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Reds and Spurs will have expected to both claim three points to aid their respective title-challenges. Plus, a round-up of Monday and Tuesday's goals

programme.title: Highlander (1986)
programme.desc: A 16th-century Scottish clansman discovers he is one of a race of immortal warriors who can only be killed by beheading, and who are destined to fight to the death until just one survives. Centuries later in modern-day New York, he prepares for a final showdown with his deadliest enemy. Fantasy action adventure, starring Christopher Lambert, Sean Connery, Clancy Brown and Roxanne Hart. Featuring a soundtrack by Queen

programme.title: Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa (2013)
programme.desc: The hapless local radio DJ ends up in the centre of a crisis when a colleague gets fired by the station's new owners, only to respond by returning to the studio with a shotgun and taking hostages. The police enlist Alan's aid as a negotiator, giving him the chance to revive his own flagging public profile. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Colm Meaney and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Home (2015)
programme.desc: An alien on the run from his own people arrives on Earth and embarks on an adventure with a daring young woman. It turns out to be a life-changing trip during which they begin to understand the true meaning of the word home. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin and Jennifer Lopez

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Grandpa's Great Escape
programme.desc: Jack's grandfather was a Second World War flying ace who now sadly has Alzheimer's disease, and when his family can no longer look after him he is moved to Twilight Towers care home, run by matron Miss Dandy. It soon becomes clear she is running the place for her own ulterior motives and it is up to Jack to help his grandpa make a daring escape and once again take to the skies in a Spitfire. Adaptation of David Walliams' children's book, starring Tom Courtenay, Kit Connor and Jennifer Saunders

programme.title: The Highway Rat
programme.desc: A ravenous rat craves buns, biscuits and all sweet things and as he tears along the highway, he is on the lookout for sugary treats to steal, but his sweet tooth leads him toward a sticky end. Rob Brydon narrates the animated comedy based on the book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler, with the voices of David Tennant, Nina Sosanya, Tom Hollander and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Stick Man
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It follows the title character on an incredible adventure across the seasons as he runs from a playful dog, gets thrown into a river, escapes from a swan's nest and even ends up on top of a fire. Will he ever get back to his family in time for Christmas? Martin Freeman heads the voice cast as Stick Man, with Hugh Bonneville, Russell Tovey, Rob Brydon and Sally Hawkins, plus Jennifer Saunders as the narrator

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (2017)
programme.desc: A prince is transformed into a hideous monster as punishment for his selfishness, and can only regain his human form by finding true love. Salvation comes in the form of a spirited village girl, who offers to take her father's place when he is imprisoned in the monster's castle. But as romance blossoms between the unlikely pair, a jealous rival suitor only sees a beast to be slain. Live action remake of the Disney animated fairytale, starring Emma Watson, Dan Stevens and Luke Evans, with Ewan McGregor, Emma Thompson and Ian McKellen playing the Beast's entourage of living ornaments

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo present some of the best music of the year, featuring Alfie Templeman, Arlo Parks, Becky Hill, DJ Nathan Dawe featuring KSI and Ella Henderson, Sigala and James Arthur, Yungblud, and Wes Nelson featuring Hardy Caprio. They also relive performances by Alicia Keys, Michael Kiwanuka and more

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Paralympians Hannah Cockcroft and Nathan Maguire, father-and-son presenting duo Martin and Roman Kemp and singer Fleur East and her blogger sister Keshia show off their music knowledge by guessing the songs after hearing a few seconds of each track. The winning pair get the chance to win up to £10,000 for a charity of their choice - but the longer they take, the more they lose from the jackpot. Hosted by Marvin and Rochelle Humes

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this second special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker and actress Sally Lindsay teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the residents of Walford prepare to welcome in the new year, things are not well in the Carter household. Consumed with emotions about his past, Mick finds himself struggling to cope with the enormity of the situation. As he battles with past demons, he begins to crumble

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The second of two festive shows sees The Vivienne, Lesley Joseph, Sabrina Grant and Sally Phillips competing to show off their dress-making skills. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a child's party skirt and then turn old clothes into a recognisable pantomime character in the transformation challenge. They then create a made-to-measure party dress that evokes memories of a particular New Year's Eve - with surprising results

programme.title: The Big New Year's In
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness kicks off the countdown to 2021 with a star-studded live extravaganza promising fun, music and games. Viewers are also invited to play along with a hilarious quiz looking back at an extraordinary 12 months

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham has a star-studded line-up for his final show of the year, including Hollywood everyman Tom Hanks, talking about his new Western News of the World, and Emily Blunt and Jamie Dornan, who have teamed up in the romantic drama Wild Mountain Thyme - a film shoe trailer has already caused a stir on the internet due to the actors' questionable Irish accents. Jessica Chastain gives the lowdown on her new spy thriller The 355, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall has advice on better eating and Nish Kumar gives his satirical take on the past 12 months. Rounding off the show, Sophie Ellis-Bextor - whose online kitchen disco shows proved a hit this summer - performs Crying at the Discotheque

programme.title: Alicia Keys Rocks New Year's Eve
programme.desc: The multi-award-winning singer-songwriter Alicia Keys takes to the stage to perform many of her hits and plenty more besides in this special concert, recorded on location in Los Angeles and building up to the imminent arrival of 2021

programme.title: Happy New Year Live!
programme.desc: The chimes of Big Ben, followed by this year's alternative to the annual London fireworks, which have been cancelled due to the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: Alicia Keys Rocks New Year's Eve
programme.desc: The celebrations continue as Alicia and her band perform more of her greatest hits to welcome in the new year

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Festive episode of the popular Scottish comedy, in which Jack and Victor face the prospect of a gloomy Hogmanay stuck in the lift at Osprey Heights, and console themselves by recalling New Years past. As the clock ticks down to 2007, Winston is also in a reminiscent mood, and takes refuge in memories of a legendary holiday season when Craigland really knew how to throw a party. Ford Kiernan, Greg Hemphill and Paul Riley star

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel to Kulm Hotel St Moritz. This Swiss Alpine hotel was the birthplace of winter tourism more than 150 years ago. Back then the owner made a bet with summer guests that if they returned in the winter and did not enjoy themselves he would pay for their stay. The guests stayed for the season - and a new industry of winter leisure and sports was born. During their stay, Giles works as a bellboy alongside veteran head concierge Silvio, while Monica helps joint managing director Jenny transform her office into a private dining room for a nostalgic candlelit dinner

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams prepare to leave the checkpoint in Cafayate, Argentina, except for Dom and Lizzie, whose disastrous last leg has left them more than 24 hours behind their competitors. The next destination is the Brazilian island of Ilha Grande, halfway between Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paolo, and at 3,600 kilometres, it is the longest stage of the journey. Emon and Jamiul decide to travel through Paraguay, while Jen and Rob decide they need to see more of the continent, and stop off at the famous Iguazu Falls

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Woody the St Bernard moves in with five-year-old Dexter, who has cerebral palsy. Dexter's family, in Surrey, are hoping that the little pup will become his companion and learn to carry out simple assistance tasks. In Hull, Mungo joins Alastair, Daron and their possessive older cocker spaniel Milo - but the dogs clash from day one, and Alastair and Daron call on Louise to help

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan follows a family of wild black bears for a year. He arrives in the forests of Minnesota in spring to observe the animals emerging from their dens. However, before he can start filming, he faces the challenge of earning their trust and overcoming his own fear of the creatures, which have a dangerous reputation

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin offers advice to house hunters who want to move to the lively market town of Abergavenny in Monmouthshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of experts compete as veterans Thomas Forrester, Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss take on more recent additions Tim Weeks, Danny Sebastian and Roo Irvine. As a team they each have one hour and £300 to buys three items to take to auction, but to turn up the heat they will also be shopping on their own in a relay. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Port Lockroy on Goudier Island in the British Antarctic Territory is the site of one of the world's most remote post offices, which has a staff of four and is open to visiting tourists during the Antarctic summer. They share the neighbourhood with more than 3,000 gentoo penguins, and this documentary follows the trials and tribulations of the birds as they try to raise their chicks in these unusual surroundings. Juliet Stevenson narrates

programme.title: Love & Friendship (2016)
programme.desc: Lady Susan Vernon arrives at her brother-in-law's residence, Churchill, with a matchmaking plan for her daughter, having left a trail of confusion behind her at her last residence, Manwaring. While young Reginald de Courcey is charmed by her, some of her family are deeply suspicious of her motives. But few people are equal to her schemes. Period comedy based on Jane Austen's novella Lady Susan, starring Kate Beckinsale, Chloe Sevigny and Stepheny Fry

programme.title: Emma (1996)
programme.desc: A privileged, headstrong young woman, who believes she is an expert in matters of the heart, sets about matchmaking between her friends - little realising she is falling in love herself and risks missing out on a chance of happiness. Period drama adapted from Jane Austen's novel, starring Gwyneth Paltrow, Ewan McGregor, Jeremy Northam, Toni Collette, Greta Scacchi and Juliet Stevenson

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: In the heart of south-east Asia lies the tropical island of Borneo. Twice the size of the British Isles, it is the third largest island on earth and home to possibly the greatest diversity of life of any island. Its huge variety of habitats, from bustling coral reefs and ancient jungles to towering mountains, have given rise to more than 60,000 species of plants and animals - many found nowhere else on Earth. This documentary covers Borneo's rich abundance of wildlife, from frogs to orang-utans

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin looks back on the making of Pole to Pole, in which he travelled along a single line of longitude across the USSR, Africa and finally into Antarctica. Along the way, he paid a visit to Chernobyl and gained an insight into the last days of the Soviet Union. His travels across Africa led to an encounter with some fellow globetrotters from his home town of Sheffield - but he was forced to make a detour when his planned route passed through a war zone

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the first of four festive episodes of the quiz show. Two favourite family teams from previous series, the Edwards and the Wrights, return for a family-themed episode. They put their patience and lateral thinking to the test as they strive to make connections between things that at first do not appear to be linked

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the last first round match, with teams of famous alumni from New College Oxford and Reading competing for a place in the semi-finals. The New College team includes political broadcaster Michael Crick and writer and playwright Christopher Hampton, while Newsnight culture correspondent Stephen Smith and film and TV critic Rhianna Dhillon are playing for Reading

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The world of celebrity has been transformed since the turn of the century, driven by some of the most dramatic technological and cultural developments in recent times. The first episode examines how the Beckhams changed the model of the celebrity power couple, while the rise of reality TV led to a gigantic influx of people gaining overnight fame, fed by a press that saw an easy source of material. The programme also examines how leaked sex tapes changed the nature of celebrity scandal

programme.title: Take That: We've Come a Long Way
programme.desc: As Take That mark their 30th year, this programme celebrates one of Britain's best-loved boy bands, with help from fans from all over the world sharing their stories of how the group touched their lives. Robbie Williams rejoins Gary Barlow, Howard Donald and Mark Owen to share favourite memories, there's a glimpse of preparations for their anniversary album, and footage of them in the studio with Bee Gees veteran Barry Gibb

programme.title: The Story of Pulp's Common People
programme.desc: Documentary examining the origins of the indie band's song about class differences in the 1990s. Frontman Jarvis Cocker returns to St Martin's College, where he met the girl who became the inspiration for the track's heroine, while the band recall the single's success. With contributions from Sadie Frost, Vic Reeves, Franz Ferdinand and Kaiser Chiefs

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by Jon Richardson

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests, including Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Happy New Year, Colin Burstead (2018)
programme.desc: A man plans to prove his worth to his extended family by throwing them a lavish New Year party, and rents a stately home in the country for the occasion. However, his status as the man of the hour is threatened by the unexpected reappearance of his estranged brother after a five-year absence, a development that throws all the relationships in the family into upheaval. Comedy directed by Ben Wheatley (High Rise, Sightseers), starring Neil Maskell, Sam Riley, Hayley Squires, Charles Dance and Doon Mackichan

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: The final documentary looks at one of the greatest challenges faced by modern Elizabethans - the loss of Britain's manufacturing heartlands and the surprising impact it has had on the state of the nation. Andrew Marr selects a rich cast of characters, from Christopher Cockerell to Clive Sinclair, who in his opinion have responded to the challenges and found ingenious ways of adapting to the changing industrial landscape

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The remaining four chefs embark on the penultimate round of the competition, cooking for some of the most respected chefs in the UK at the Chefs Table. The chefs have three hours and 15 minutes to create a spectacular dish and get it to the pass ready to serve, hoping to wow the judges and earn praise from the best in the business. In the second round, the competitors are challenged to create a dish of their own design dedicated to someone special in their life, before one of them is sent home

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Our Dementia Choir with Vicky McClure
programme.desc: Just over two years ago, Vicky McClure took to the stage at the Royal Concert Hall in Nottingham with her choir of people all living with dementia to perform in front of 2000 people. Now, this heart-warming programme tells the story of Vicky dementia choir through the Covid-19 lockdown, leading up to a very special Christmas performance for one choir member

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: In this review of the year 2020, Yvonne and Julian look back at some positive moments, some incredibly important conversations and end it on a happy note. With voiceover

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel more than 120 miles north of the Arctic Circle into Swedish Lapland to work in Icehotel in the village of Jukkasjarvi. Using two-ton blocks of ice hacked from the nearby River Torne, Giles works with a team of international artists to create individually crafted rooms and is taught how to make drinking glasses from ice, while Monica learns how the head chef uses natural ingredients found in the nearby forest; and meets Manne, an 82-year-old man who supplies reindeer meat to the hotel

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After a frantic race to make it to Ilha Grande, the teams set off for the next checkpoint in Mendoza, Western Argentina, but as the race enters its final phase, the depleted budgets begin to take their toll on the competitors. Emon and Jamiul are the first to set off, but disagree about the best route to take. Jen and Rob are the team with the most money remaining, but also have clashing priorities over how best to proceed. Jo and Sam look for jobs that include bed and board to save their money, while Dom and Lizzie hitch a ride and receive an unexpected invitation

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Shih tzu-pug cross Miska moves on to a houseboat with new owner Scarlett. After signs that Miska is adapting well to her new home, Scarlett takes her to work, but she is shocked when the dog ignores her and starts yapping at her colleagues in the open-plan office. In Surrey, the Summerscale-Heards continue training St Bernard pup Woody to offer assistance to five-year-old Dexter, and in West Yorkshire, Phoebe the cavapoo joins retirees Linda and Andrew - only to start nipping and jumping up

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota in the summer to catch up with the black bears, but is upset to discover that cub Hope has been abandoned by her mother. With the young animal facing starvation, he is forced to step in and become a surrogate parent, and balances his new duties with continued study of the rest of the family

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman visits the seaside town of Ayr to help buyers with £400,000 to spend, who have pinpointed Ayr as a potential location due to its close proximity to the beach and city

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: From Epsom Racecourse in Surrey, where the blues get into a squabble, the reds spend big, and Anita Manning looks at a curious Victorian gold pencil

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Port Lockroy on Goudier Island in the British Antarctic Territory is the site of one of the world's most remote post offices, which has a staff of four and is open to visiting tourists during the Antarctic summer. They share the neighbourhood with more than 3,000 gentoo penguins, and this documentary follows the trials and tribulations of the birds as they try to raise their chicks in these unusual surroundings. Juliet Stevenson narrates

programme.title: To Catch a Thief (1955)
programme.desc: A retired cat burglar becomes the prime suspect for a series of robberies carried out in his own distinctive style, and sets out to clear his name by catching the copycat crook responsible. His quest takes him to the Cote d'Azur, where he encounters the demure daughter of a wealthy holiday-maker, and before long the two are drawn together. Alfred Hitchcock's Oscar-winning romantic mystery, starring Cary Grant, Grace Kelly and Jessie Royce Landis

programme.title: North By Northwest (1959)
programme.desc: A suave advertising executive is mistaken for an American intelligence agent by a foreign spy and finds himself drawn into a world of espionage and adventure, leading to a high-tension cross-country chase made more perilous when the FBI intervenes. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller, starring Cary Grant, James Mason, Eva Marie Saint, Leo G Carroll and Martin Landau

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: A look across Hawaii's varied islands, discovering how they were made, and how its wildlife thrives. The most remote island chain on Earth, its tropical shores are hard to reach. However, for the hardy creatures that have made it there, such as the waterfall-climbing fish, carnivorous caterpillar and Laysan albatross, a land of opportunity awaits. From newly formed lava fields, to lush jungles, and vibrant coral reefs, these diverse and beautiful islands have it all. As this documentary reveals, its people are looking to the future and attempting to protect and foster its flora and fauna

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin recalls the making of his third travel series Full Circle, a journey around the Pacific Rim which took him to Russia, China, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand and North and South America. This series saw Michael adopting a more investigative role than in previous odysseys, particularly when encountering the drug wars and poverty that plagued Colombia. With contributions from David Attenborough and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as Casino de Monte Carlo holds an exclusive event with a Prohibition theme aimed at high rollers as it seeks to attract new clients, while at the palace Prince Albert II hosts the new French ambassador. In Monaco's only tattoo parlour, Princess Stephanie's daughter Camille Gottlieb is getting a discreet new inking and sharing what life is like for her in the principality, and four billion euros' worth of motor vessels enter the harbour for the annual yacht show

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents as two favourite teams from previous series, one from the north and one from the South, compete in the quiz show. The contestants put their lateral thinking to the test as they strive to make connections between things that at first do not appear to be linked at first

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as two teams, each made up of four graduates, compete on behalf of their former universities in the first semi-final of the academic quiz's festive series

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: Charting the story of celebrity in the late noughties, the second episode looks at how different groups cashed in on the public's insatiable appetite for access to the famous. Young celebrities were commoditised, criticised and discarded by the people who profited from them, until a new breed of celebrity emerged, ready to wrestle control back for themselves. As the demand for the public to have access to their idols' every move increased, celebrity magazine circulation exploded, and celebrities found themselves up for scrutiny by a new type of showbiz journalism

programme.title: The Nine Lives of Ozzy Osbourne (2020)
programme.desc: Biography tracing the rock musician's life from his childhood in poverty and spell in prison for burglary, to fronting legendary metal group Black Sabbath and subsequent Grammy Award-winning solo career. More recently, Ozzy has become rock's elder statesman and a loveable family figure. Contributors include wife Sharon Osbourne, son and daughter Jack and Kelly Osbourne, and fellow musician Ice-T

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Tom Allen guest stars on the topical comedy show hosted by Romesh Ranganathan, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: In this review of the year 2020, Yvonne and Julian look back at some positive moments, some incredibly important conversations and end it on a happy note. With voiceover

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Cherry Healey and Tyger Drew-Honey to take a look at the year's cultural highlights, including the first glimpse of new singing talent Adele on Jools Holland's Later, the Spice Girls reuniting, and Shilpa Shetty on the receiving end of racist abuse from her housemates in Celebrity Big Brother

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: The rains hit the waterhole, and for the first time in half a year, grass begins to grow and water is everywhere, dramatically changing the fortunes of the wild residents. The waterhole area is totally transformed into a lush grazing pasture and a pair of Egyptian geese take up permanent residence on the waterhole's edge. Using remote cameras, Chris Packham reveals how the waterhole hyena clan copes with these new circumstances, while Ella Al-Shamahi uncovers the huge shift in the waterhole timetable and spots new competition flooding into the area - wildebeest

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The search for a chef to be crowned MasterChef: Professionals champion 2020 is almost over, as the three finalists face one last challenge. They are given three hours and 15 minutes to cook a starter, a main course and a dessert, each of which takes their culinary abilities to a whole new level, with flawless presentation and exciting flavours. Judges Marcus Wareing, Monica Galetti and Gregg Wallace assess their efforts, before deciding the winner

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Woody the St Bernard moves in with five-year-old Dexter, who has cerebral palsy. Dexter's family, in Surrey, are hoping that the little pup will become his companion and learn to carry out simple assistance tasks. In Hull, Mungo joins Alastair, Daron and their possessive older cocker spaniel Milo - but the dogs clash from day one, and Alastair and Daron call on Louise to help

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit Schloss Elmau, a hotel located deep in the snowy Bavarian Alps that is promoted as providing a cultural hideaway that allows guests the chance to restore their mind, body and soul. Monica joins in one of the meditation sessions held every day for the restaurant staff, and Giles recites poetry and travels to a mountain hut to serve dinner, while dressed in lederhosen. They join the hotel guests for an evening performance featuring world-renowned musicians playing for free

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams embark on the final leg of their journey, heading off on the 4,800 kilometre trek to the finish line in Ushuaia, Argentina, the most southerly city in the world. However, as they prepare to leave the last checkpoint, one of the teams reveals that they have run out of money and will not be completing the challenge. The three remaining pairs rely on different tactics to get ahead, benefiting from risky gambles, lucky opportunities and the kindness of strangers

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Linus the Bedlington terrier joins the Seymour family, but mum Rebecca is not sure she has made the right decision. Cookie the French bulldog moves in with hairdresser Louise, personal trainer Phil and their two young boys. The family have high hopes she will fit in with their active lifestyle, but are thrown when she refuses to go out on walks. In n West Yorkshire, Phoebe the cavapoo has now been with retirees Linda and Andrew for a month and shows no signs of slowing down

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota at the beginning of autumn to check how abandoned bear cub Hope is progressing and is delighted to discover that her life has taken a turn for the better. However, a fresh danger is posed by the arrival of hunters in the forest and Gordon decides to walk with the animals to protect them

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin serves up some winning houses for tennis-playing town-seekers keen to test out Shaftesbury as their perfect Dorset town and have £460,000 to make the move. They quickly discover it's a top choice, and not just because it's on a hill but because of a great social scene, high street independents, festival events and stunning green spaces

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss are on hand as two teams test their knowledge of antiques at the Lincoln Antiques Fair. Host Charlie Ross also hears about a devastating extreme weather event that destroyed nearby Louth in 1920

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham finds out about the much-loved dolphin that has started breaking people's bones and reveals how a tiny crustacean has mastered the holy grail of science - the art of invisibility

programme.title: Rio Bravo (1959)
programme.desc: Sheriff John T Chance and his drunken deputy Dude make a courageous stand against a rancher and his hired guns, who are threatening to tear apart a peace-loving town if a murderer being held in the local jail is not released. Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Dean Martin, Ricky Nelson, Walter Brennan and Angie Dickinson, who won a Golden Globe for Most Promising Newcomer for her portrayal of Feathers

programme.title: Paint Your Wagon (1969)
programme.desc: A gold prospector buys a wife from a Mormon and agrees to share her with his mining partner when she insists they all live together. Mindful that her presence in an all-male town might give rise to jealousy, he hijacks a stagecoach carrying six working girls and opens a brothel in town. Oscar-nominated musical Western, starring Lee Marvin, Clint Eastwood, with Jean Seberg and Harve Presnell

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael reflects on his fourth travel series, exploring the Sahara. This journey brought him into contact with nomads, refugees and artists who had sought out this vast desert, but also those displaced by war, the migrant crisis and the rise of militant Islam. The journey took Michael over the Atlas Mountains, to a Smara refugee camp, to the famed city of Timbuktu, and into the deep desert as part of a camel train

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: A tour of an extraordinary new home, Villa Troglodyte, which has come onto the market. Built as if carved out of the rock, the price is 'only' £23million because it doesn't have a sea view. The pressure to build more houses in such a small country has left Prince Albert with a challenge. Cameras follow him as he attends meetings about a huge two-billion-euro land extension project he has initiated. Extending into the sea is a complex operation, but once complete the largest villas there are estimated to sell for around 100million euros

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Six favourite contestants from previous series return for a one-off special - all themed around doctors. The battle is on as a team of NHS professionals take on a trio of Doctor Who fans in a competition to make the connections between things that, at first glance, seem utterly random. Hosted by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Two teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the second semi-final of the academic quiz's festive series. Jeremy Paxman hosts

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The documentary charting the transformation of celebrity examines how people capitalised on the digital revolution in order to sidestep the traditional routes to fame. When the old tabloid machinery began to lose power, celebrities made the move to the digital press - and soon after pop acts were making a buzz on the internet. Meanwhile, structured reality shows dominated the younger demographic - and all of these things came together through social media. All of a sudden there was a new way of becoming famous

programme.title: Before They Were Famous
programme.desc: Another collection of archive clips showing celebrities' embarrassing early screen appearances

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain, regular panellist Hugh Dennis and many of the comedians who have appeared this year mark the end of 2020 with a special show looking back on the year's events, along with unseen material, out-takes and all the usual fun

programme.title: Jools' Annual Hootenanny
programme.desc: The piano man returns with his annual new year music show, although like most things this year, there are a few changes. Jools and his Rhythm and Blues Orchestra are joined by artists including Celeste, Tom Jones, Chris Difford, Michael Kiwanuka, Roisin Murphy, Ruby Turner and Rick Wakeman, and there are archive clips of Hootenanny performances from the likes of Jamiroquai, Rudimental and Ella Eyre, Madness, ABC and Aloe Blacc

programme.title: Glastonbury Tea Time Legends
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe and Jo Whiley introduce some of the best performances from the Glastonbury Festival's regular Sunday evening legends slot, including Kylie Minogue's acclaimed set from the 2019 festival. Other artists featured include Neil Diamond, Brian Wilson, Shirley Bassey, Lionel Richie and Al Green

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A celebration of the best of Top of the Pops in 1988, including performances by S'Express, Bomb the Bass, Enya, Maxi Priest, Everything But the Girl, the Proclaimers, Neneh Cherry, and Eurythmics

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Robin Hood's Bay, the fishing village on the North Yorkshire coast, revealing tales of smuggling intrigue and bravery

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint an intriguing yet simple painting of an old log cabin nestled deep in the snowy woods

programme.title: Royal Institution Christmas Lectures 2020
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerski reveals how shifting ocean water distributes heat and nutrients around the Earth. This process is linked to almost every aspect of human existence, and the lecture explores how dependent we are on the ocean for weather, food supply and connection between land masses - as well as exploring what needs to be done by humans to maintain this vital element of the Earth's ecosystem

programme.title: Bruce Dickinson: Scream for Me Sarajevo (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about a 1994 concert by Bruce Dickinson and his band Skunkworks in Sarajevo, when the capital of Bosnia and Herzegovina was in its third year under siege during the ongoing Bosnian War. Featuring personal accounts of the band members who risked their lives to perform in a war zone, and the fans who attended the concert while living in the midst of continual violence

programme.title: Metal Britannia
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the genre's emergence in the late 1960s, pioneered by bands including Black Sabbath and Judas Priest, who were based in the industrialised Midlands. This classic form of metal was challenged by punk in the late 1970s, before a new wave of groups such as Iron Maiden, Def Leppard and Saxon reinvigorated the style. Featuring contributions by singers Ian Gillan, Glenn Tipton, Lemmy, Bruce Dickinson and Rob Halford, guitarist Tony Iommi and keyboard player Jon Lord. Narrated by Nigel Planer

programme.title: Metallica at Glastonbury 2014
programme.desc: A chance to see the band's headline set on the Pyramid Stage at the 2014 event - the first time a heavy metal group had topped the bill at the famous festival. Throughout their 90-minute set they played tracks from their back catalogue including Nothing Else Matters, Enter Sandman, Seek and Destroy plus Thin Lizzy's Whiskey in the Jar

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint an intriguing yet simple painting of an old log cabin nestled deep in the snowy woods

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Robin Hood's Bay, the fishing village on the North Yorkshire coast, revealing tales of smuggling intrigue and bravery

programme.title: Royal Institution Christmas Lectures 2020
programme.desc: The first of three lectures on the hidden wonders of Earth and the impact of human activity, with Professor Chris Jackson charting the planet's changing climate. He shows how the oldest rocks and fossils provide evidence of radical climate changes throughout its history, revealing that what drives them is the amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Walberswick, the former medieval port on the Suffolk coast, which is now a favourite retreat for writers, artists and day trippers. For centuries the village lurched from prosperity to poverty and in medieval times a bitter battle for supremacy was fought against the neighbouring port of Dunwich

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a scene which marks the line between home and the edge of a winter forest

programme.title: Royal Institution Christmas Lectures 2020
programme.desc: In the final episode, Dr Tara Shine explores the gases that make up the air we breathe, one of which may provide the answer to transport in the future

programme.title: Norman Wisdom: His Story
programme.desc: The life and career of the comedy actor, told by those who knew him well, from his son, daughter and daughter-in-law to famous friends including Stephen Frears, Ricky Tomlinson, Leslie Phillips, Honor Blackman and Vera Lynn

programme.title: Uncle Vanya
programme.desc: Recording of the acclaimed 2020 production of Chekhov's play at London's Harold Pinter Theatre, which was filmed for streaming under lockdown conditions after being cancelled due to Covid-19. Toby Jones plays the title role of the manager of a remote rural estate where the simple, ordered existence is disrupted by the arrival of an aged academic and his younger wife. As he becomes desperately infatuated with her, she in turn is drawn to a local doctor consumed by his own personal demons. With Richard Armitage and Roger Allam

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright presents a special helping of party tunes for New Year's Eve, featuring performances by the Three Degrees, Elton John, Joan Jett and the Blackhearts, Europe, Blondie, the Nolans, Lionel Richie , the Bee Gees, Madonna and Kylie Minogue

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a scene which marks the line between home and the edge of a winter forest

For centuries the village lurched from prosperity to poverty and in medieval times a bitter battle for supremacy was fought against the neighbouring port of Dunwich

programme.title: Royal Institution Christmas Lectures 2020
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerski reveals how shifting ocean water distributes heat and nutrients around the Earth. This process is linked to almost every aspect of human existence, and the lecture explores how dependent we are on the ocean for weather, food supply and connection between land masses - as well as exploring what needs to be done by humans to maintain this vital element of the Earth's ecosystem

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of the Northumberland village of Warkworth. With the help of locals he discovers clues that point back almost 1,000 years to the Norman conquest, revealing how the invaders laid the foundations of a planned community still visible to this day

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross produces an image of a lonely cabin comfortably nestled on the edge of a glass-smooth frozen pond

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A mix of nostalgia and music, featuring archive material from the Top of the Pops studio and pop videos

programme.title: Dawn French Live: 30 Million Minutes
programme.desc: The celebrated comedian's critically acclaimed one-woman show, filmed during its final West End run in 2016. Dawn takes her audience on a journey through her life experiences, detailing irksome tribulations, crucial lessons that she has learned along the way, and things 'she knows to be true'. With a sharp eye for comic detail and an ear for the absurdities of life, Dawn draws on her personal and professional personas to offer an alternative view to modern living

programme.title: Mr Stink (2012)
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville stars, with Sheridan Smith, Johnny Vegas and Isabella Blake-Thomas, and an appearance from Britain's Got Talent winner Pudsey the dog

Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is having his first ever sleepover night with Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny in a tent in his garden, and to make it extra special, he buys a snooze cloud pillow, which has double the bounce and triple the comfort. Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny arrives, and it is soon clear they do things very differently. Love Monster has a night-time hot chocolate, while Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny drinks cold carrot juice. She brushes her teeth, and he flosses. Love Monster likes a bedtime story, while Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny prefers music. This confuses Love Monster - he thought everyone does the same things at bedtime. Then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny starts snoring and talking in her sleep and grabbing all the covers. They both end up on the floor and decide there isn't room for both of them on the snooze-cloud. Love Monster goes to his bedroom, and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny stays in the tent. But later that night, Love Monster decides to give it another try and makes her cold carrot juice, and she makes h

programme.title: YolanDa's Band Jam
programme.desc: Featuring rock superstars Feeder, who perform some of their songs.

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams enjoy a special pirate party as Swashbucklers Tanay, Freya, Jonas and Josiah tackle some physical games to retrieve Gem's jewels. Meanwhile, Mr Bloom and Nina make a treasure chest out of egg cartons, and Robert and Cook prepare a tasty treat for all to enjoy

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The four adventure-seeking superheroes visit London on New Year's Eve, but Big Ben is missing

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
