programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major Clanger invents a knitting machine, thinking this will save Granny ever having to make the effort to knit again. However, Granny likes knitting and when she sees the hideous garments the machine produces she challenges it to a great knit-off to determine who is the best knitter. Narrated by Michael Palin

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos play hide-and-seek, while the Pontipines go for a walk around the garden and meet their neighbours the Wottingers

programme.title: Hushabye Lullabye
programme.desc: Dillie Dally follows the night-time stars to discover the planets.

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Join Tom Hardy for tonight's bedtime story about two friends who find a new way to keep in touch despite being far apart.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Edne's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Mr Stink (2012)
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville stars, with Sheridan Smith, Johnny Vegas and Isabella Blake-Thomas, and an appearance from Britain's Got Talent winner Pudsey the dog

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Frozen (2013)
programme.desc: A princess with the power to freeze anything she touches loses control of her abilities and plunges the kingdom into a perpetual winter. She goes into hiding, but her sister remains determined that she can be saved from her own magic and sets off on a quest to find her, aided by a mountain man and a living snowman. Disney animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kristen Bell and Idina Menzel

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present another edition of their unorthodox, star-filled quiz show. This time, all the contestants are from the world of music, and as befits the time of year, the entry requirement is that they must have released a successful Christmas hit. Stop the Cavalry singer Jona Lewie teams up with Mike Batt, who released Wombling Merry Christmas, while 1972 Christmas Number One hit-maker Jimmy Osmond pairs with his 2015 counterpart - X Factor's Ben Haenow. Choirmaster Gareth Malone joins forces with Samantha Stevenson of the Military Wives Choir, and Justin and Dan Hawkins of The Darkness also compete, before playing out the show with their festive favourite Christmas Time (Don't Let the Bells End)

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick is desperate to help Denise and takes matters into his own hands - although he may have only made matters worse. Ruby warns Stacey to stay out of her marriage, while Jay makes out that the kiss with Honey was a mistake - is he hiding his true feelings?

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first of two special Christmas episodes of the culinary challenge, in which eight finalists and semi-finalists from past years return to face a festive test of their skills. The first episode features Danny Parker from the 2014 series, 2017's Louisa Ellis, 2009's Marianne Lumb, and 2018 semi-finalist William Chilila. Monica Galetti sets them the task of making festive canapés, before they move on to the dreaded skills test and a seasonally themed two-course menu

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Adela and Father Roberts prove unlikely allies for Ruth, forcing Clodagh to impose strict order or risk losing control of the convent, as well as herself. But events escalate as Dilip Rai and Kanchi are discovered together, with dramatic consequences. Ruth flees the palace to declare her love to Mr Dean, who is defending the convent against retaliation from the village. Conclusion of the adaptation of Rumer Godden's novel, starring Gemma Arterton and Alessandro Nivola

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford and Brighton & Hove Albion v Arsenal at Amex Stadium. United and Wolves met on four occasions in all competitions last term, with three of those contests ending level and the Red Devils prevailing 1-0 at home in an FA Cup replay in the other. Brighton did the double over the Gunners last season, winning 2-1 home and away, and will be hopeful of shocking the Londoners once again. Plus, a round-up of the rest of tonight and yesterday's fixtures

programme.title: Point Break (1991)
programme.desc: An FBI agent infiltrates a Californian surfing gang suspected of carrying out a series of armed robberies wearing masks modelled on former US presidents. However, his loyalties are tested to the limits when he is befriended by the gang's leader. Kathryn Bigelow's thriller, starring Keanu Reeves, Patrick Swayze, Gary Busey and Lori Petty

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The annual harvest festival brings the whole village together, but one resident's announcement causes unexpected emotional consequences for the community. Spoof documentary exploring modern life in the Cotswolds, written by and starring siblings Daisy May and Charlie Cooper

programme.title: Anita & Me (2002)
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Meena strikes up an unlikely friendship with blonde teenage tearaway Anita. Despite their ethnic and social differences, the girls manage to support each other through difficult times while their families cope with life's problems. Comedy drama set in a Midlands village in the 1970s and based on the novel by Meera Syal, starring Chandeep Uppal, Anna Brewster, Max Beesley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kathy Burke, Lynn Redgrave and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Strange Magic (2015)
programme.desc: A fairy living in an enchanted forest tries to rescue her sister from a monstrous creature. Meanwhile, the magical kingdom is thrown into chaos by a love potion that results in a series of unexpected romances. Animated fantasy musical, with the voices of Evan Rachel Wood, Alan Cumming and Kristin Chenoweth

programme.title: Early Man (2018)
programme.desc: A caveman tribe must learn to play football in order to regain their homeland from a conquering civilisation. Animation, with the voice of Eddie Redmayne

programme.title: Gangsta Granny
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Schoolboy Ben is bored witless after being made to stay with his grandma. All she ever wants to do is play Scrabble and eat yucky cabbage soup. But it turns out there's a whole lot more to this OAP than cardigans and toffees when she tells him how she used to be an international jewel thief - and when she admits there was one heist she never quite managed in her heyday, the pair set out on a criminal adventure. Julia McKenzie stars as the shady senior citizen, with Walliams and Miranda Hart as the boys' ballroom-dancing parents, alongside Joanna Lumley, Rob Brydon and Robbie Williams, with Reece Buttery as Ben

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Room on the Broom
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It tells the story of a kind witch who invites a surprising number of animals to ride on her broom - much to the frustration of her cat. But when a fearsome dragon appears, what will the gang do? Gillian Anderson heads the voice cast as the witch, with Rob Brydon, Martin Clunes, Sally Hawkins and David Walliams as the animals, Timothy Spall as the dragon and Simon Pegg as narrator

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake

programme.title: Dragons: Gift of the Night Fury
programme.desc: Animated short film featuring the characters from How to Train Your Dragon, in which the Vikings prepare to celebrate their winter festival, only to find all the winged reptiles have mysteriously disappeared. Hiccup tries to discover where they have gone, and stumbles on a mysterious island. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Young Viking Hiccup has successfully persuaded his people to live in peace with dragons, but now faces a new threat from a warlord who is capturing the flying lizards to build an army. As Hiccup tries to keep the peace, he is reunited with his long-lost mother. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Jay Baruchel, Gerard Butler and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host another sport-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Famous faces Scarlett Moffatt, Matt Everitt, Lucrezia Millarini and Stephen K Amos are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of The Sitcom Bottom, Glastonbury Festival, Die Hard and Fela Kuti

programme.title: Aled Jones at 50
programme.desc: As Aled Jones turns 50, this special programme looks back at his life and celebrates his remarkable musical career since making a name for himself as a young soprano. Throughout the programme, Aled discovers archive of himself as a boy he had no idea existed, and also reflects on some of the highlights of his career, singing for Pope John Paul, the Royal family and at the wedding of Bob Geldof and Paula Yates

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four of the most memorable professional chefs from past series - Steve Barringer, Exose Grant Lopo-Ndigna, Oli Martin and Olivia Burt - are invited to return to the MasterChef kitchen to compete in a festive-themed special. First, the chefs have the chance to relive the much-feared Skills Test, and catch up with judges on how their careers have progressed since taking part in the competition. Later, they are tasked to create a seasonally themed two-course menu in two-and-half hours to impress the judges

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy plan some New Year's Eve festivities in this Christmas special, with both inviting their parents to join them. Things soon go badly wrong as party games give way to an uncompromising round of guests pointing out what New Year's resolutions everyone needs to make - with a distinct lack of festive spirit on display. Starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Pandemonium
programme.desc: One-off video diary comedy, in which a boy documents his family's disastrous year. With their summer holiday to California having been cancelled and their finances devastated due to the pandemic, family morale is at an all-time low. But the Jessops make one final stab at trying to salvage something from 2020 with a trip to Margate in the middle of December

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Newcastle United v Liverpool at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Fulham at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Reds and Spurs will have expected to both claim three points to aid their respective title-challenges. Plus, a round-up of Monday and Tuesday's goals

programme.title: Highlander (1986)
programme.desc: A 16th-century Scottish clansman discovers he is one of a race of immortal warriors who can only be killed by beheading, and who are destined to fight to the death until just one survives. Centuries later in modern-day New York, he prepares for a final showdown with his deadliest enemy. Fantasy action adventure, starring Christopher Lambert, Sean Connery, Clancy Brown and Roxanne Hart. Featuring a soundtrack by Queen

programme.title: Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa (2013)
programme.desc: The hapless local radio DJ ends up in the centre of a crisis when a colleague gets fired by the station's new owners, only to respond by returning to the studio with a shotgun and taking hostages. The police enlist Alan's aid as a negotiator, giving him the chance to revive his own flagging public profile. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Colm Meaney and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Home (2015)
programme.desc: An alien on the run from his own people arrives on Earth and embarks on an adventure with a daring young woman. It turns out to be a life-changing trip during which they begin to understand the true meaning of the word home. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin and Jennifer Lopez

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Grandpa's Great Escape
programme.desc: Jack's grandfather was a Second World War flying ace who now sadly has Alzheimer's disease, and when his family can no longer look after him he is moved to Twilight Towers care home, run by matron Miss Dandy. It soon becomes clear she is running the place for her own ulterior motives and it is up to Jack to help his grandpa make a daring escape and once again take to the skies in a Spitfire. Adaptation of David Walliams' children's book, starring Tom Courtenay, Kit Connor and Jennifer Saunders

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Highway Rat
programme.desc: A ravenous rat craves buns, biscuits and all sweet things and as he tears along the highway, he is on the lookout for sugary treats to steal, but his sweet tooth leads him toward a sticky end. Rob Brydon narrates the animated comedy based on the book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler, with the voices of David Tennant, Nina Sosanya, Tom Hollander and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Stick Man
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It follows the title character on an incredible adventure across the seasons as he runs from a playful dog, gets thrown into a river, escapes from a swan's nest and even ends up on top of a fire. Will he ever get back to his family in time for Christmas? Martin Freeman heads the voice cast as Stick Man, with Hugh Bonneville, Russell Tovey, Rob Brydon and Sally Hawkins, plus Jennifer Saunders as the narrator

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (2017)
programme.desc: A prince is transformed into a hideous monster as punishment for his selfishness, and can only regain his human form by finding true love. Salvation comes in the form of a spirited village girl, who offers to take her father's place when he is imprisoned in the monster's castle. But as romance blossoms between the unlikely pair, a jealous rival suitor only sees a beast to be slain. Live action remake of the Disney animated fairytale, starring Emma Watson, Dan Stevens and Luke Evans, with Ewan McGregor, Emma Thompson and Ian McKellen playing the Beast's entourage of living ornaments

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo present some of the best music of the year, featuring Alfie Templeman, Arlo Parks, Becky Hill, DJ Nathan Dawe featuring KSI and Ella Henderson, Sigala and James Arthur, Yungblud, and Wes Nelson featuring Hardy Caprio. They also relive performances by Alicia Keys, Michael Kiwanuka and more

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Paralympians Hannah Cockcroft and Nathan Maguire, father-and-son presenting duo Martin and Roman Kemp and singer Fleur East and her blogger sister Keshia show off their music knowledge by guessing the songs after hearing a few seconds of each track. The winning pair get the chance to win up to £10,000 for a charity of their choice - but the longer they take, the more they lose from the jackpot. Hosted by Marvin and Rochelle Humes

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this second special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker and actress Sally Lindsay teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the residents of Walford prepare to welcome in the new year, things are not well in the Carter household. Consumed with emotions about his past, Mick finds himself struggling to cope with the enormity of the situation. As he battles with past demons, he begins to crumble

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The second of two festive shows sees The Vivienne, Lesley Joseph, Sabrina Grant and Sally Phillips competing to show off their dress-making skills. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a child's party skirt and then turn old clothes into a recognisable pantomime character in the transformation challenge. They then create a made-to-measure party dress that evokes memories of a particular New Year's Eve - with surprising results

programme.title: The Big New Year's In
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness kicks off the countdown to 2021 with a star-studded live extravaganza promising fun, music and games. Viewers are also invited to play along with a hilarious quiz looking back at an extraordinary 12 months

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham has a star-studded line-up for his final show of the year, including Hollywood everyman Tom Hanks, talking about his new Western News of the World, and Emily Blunt and Jamie Dornan, who have teamed up in the romantic drama Wild Mountain Thyme - a film shoe trailer has already caused a stir on the internet due to the actors' questionable Irish accents. Jessica Chastain gives the lowdown on her new spy thriller The 355, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall has advice on better eating and Nish Kumar gives his satirical take on the past 12 months. Rounding off the show, Sophie Ellis-Bextor - whose online kitchen disco shows proved a hit this summer - performs Crying at the Discotheque

programme.title: Alicia Keys Rocks New Year's Eve
programme.desc: The multi-award-winning singer-songwriter Alicia Keys takes to the stage to perform many of her hits and plenty more besides in this special concert, recorded on location in Los Angeles and building up to the imminent arrival of 2021

programme.title: Happy New Year Live!
programme.desc: The chimes of Big Ben, followed by this year's alternative to the annual London fireworks, which have been cancelled due to the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: Alicia Keys Rocks New Year's Eve
programme.desc: The celebrations continue as Alicia and her band perform more of her greatest hits to welcome in the new year

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Festive episode of the popular Scottish comedy, in which Jack and Victor face the prospect of a gloomy Hogmanay stuck in the lift at Osprey Heights, and console themselves by recalling New Years past. As the clock ticks down to 2007, Winston is also in a reminiscent mood, and takes refuge in memories of a legendary holiday season when Craigland really knew how to throw a party. Ford Kiernan, Greg Hemphill and Paul Riley star

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Edne's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Mr Stink (2012)
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville stars, with Sheridan Smith, Johnny Vegas and Isabella Blake-Thomas, and an appearance from Britain's Got Talent winner Pudsey the dog

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Frozen (2013)
programme.desc: A princess with the power to freeze anything she touches loses control of her abilities and plunges the kingdom into a perpetual winter. She goes into hiding, but her sister remains determined that she can be saved from her own magic and sets off on a quest to find her, aided by a mountain man and a living snowman. Disney animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kristen Bell and Idina Menzel

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present another edition of their unorthodox, star-filled quiz show. This time, all the contestants are from the world of music, and as befits the time of year, the entry requirement is that they must have released a successful Christmas hit. Stop the Cavalry singer Jona Lewie teams up with Mike Batt, who released Wombling Merry Christmas, while 1972 Christmas Number One hit-maker Jimmy Osmond pairs with his 2015 counterpart - X Factor's Ben Haenow. Choirmaster Gareth Malone joins forces with Samantha Stevenson of the Military Wives Choir, and Justin and Dan Hawkins of The Darkness also compete, before playing out the show with their festive favourite Christmas Time (Don't Let the Bells End)

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick is desperate to help Denise and takes matters into his own hands - although he may have only made matters worse. Ruby warns Stacey to stay out of her marriage, while Jay makes out that the kiss with Honey was a mistake - is he hiding his true feelings?

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first of two special Christmas episodes of the culinary challenge, in which eight finalists and semi-finalists from past years return to face a festive test of their skills. The first episode features Danny Parker from the 2014 series, 2017's Louisa Ellis, 2009's Marianne Lumb, and 2018 semi-finalist William Chilila. Monica Galetti sets them the task of making festive canapés, before they move on to the dreaded skills test and a seasonally themed two-course menu

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Adela and Father Roberts prove unlikely allies for Ruth, forcing Clodagh to impose strict order or risk losing control of the convent, as well as herself. But events escalate as Dilip Rai and Kanchi are discovered together, with dramatic consequences. Ruth flees the palace to declare her love to Mr Dean, who is defending the convent against retaliation from the village. Conclusion of the adaptation of Rumer Godden's novel, starring Gemma Arterton and Alessandro Nivola

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford and Brighton & Hove Albion v Arsenal at Amex Stadium. United and Wolves met on four occasions in all competitions last term, with three of those contests ending level and the Red Devils prevailing 1-0 at home in an FA Cup replay in the other. Brighton did the double over the Gunners last season, winning 2-1 home and away, and will be hopeful of shocking the Londoners once again. Plus, a round-up of the rest of tonight and yesterday's fixtures

programme.title: Point Break (1991)
programme.desc: An FBI agent infiltrates a Californian surfing gang suspected of carrying out a series of armed robberies wearing masks modelled on former US presidents. However, his loyalties are tested to the limits when he is befriended by the gang's leader. Kathryn Bigelow's thriller, starring Keanu Reeves, Patrick Swayze, Gary Busey and Lori Petty

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The annual harvest festival brings the whole village together, but one resident's announcement causes unexpected emotional consequences for the community. Spoof documentary exploring modern life in the Cotswolds, written by and starring siblings Daisy May and Charlie Cooper

programme.title: Anita & Me (2002)
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Meena strikes up an unlikely friendship with blonde teenage tearaway Anita. Despite their ethnic and social differences, the girls manage to support each other through difficult times while their families cope with life's problems. Comedy drama set in a Midlands village in the 1970s and based on the novel by Meera Syal, starring Chandeep Uppal, Anna Brewster, Max Beesley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kathy Burke, Lynn Redgrave and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Strange Magic (2015)
programme.desc: A fairy living in an enchanted forest tries to rescue her sister from a monstrous creature. Meanwhile, the magical kingdom is thrown into chaos by a love potion that results in a series of unexpected romances. Animated fantasy musical, with the voices of Evan Rachel Wood, Alan Cumming and Kristin Chenoweth

programme.title: Early Man (2018)
programme.desc: A caveman tribe must learn to play football in order to regain their homeland from a conquering civilisation. Animation, with the voice of Eddie Redmayne

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the Isle of Arran, the island that has something for everyone - castles, rugged mountains, stunning wildlife and even a nudist beach

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: All the latest developments as Scotland responds to the coronavirus pandemic, plus answers to the many questions you have during these unprecedented times

programme.title: Room on the Broom
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It tells the story of a kind witch who invites a surprising number of animals to ride on her broom - much to the frustration of her cat. But when a fearsome dragon appears, what will the gang do? Gillian Anderson heads the voice cast as the witch, with Rob Brydon, Martin Clunes, Sally Hawkins and David Walliams as the animals, Timothy Spall as the dragon and Simon Pegg as narrator

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake

programme.title: Dragons: Gift of the Night Fury
programme.desc: Animated short film featuring the characters from How to Train Your Dragon, in which the Vikings prepare to celebrate their winter festival, only to find all the winged reptiles have mysteriously disappeared. Hiccup tries to discover where they have gone, and stumbles on a mysterious island. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Young Viking Hiccup has successfully persuaded his people to live in peace with dragons, but now faces a new threat from a warlord who is capturing the flying lizards to build an army. As Hiccup tries to keep the peace, he is reunited with his long-lost mother. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Jay Baruchel, Gerard Butler and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host another sport-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Famous faces Scarlett Moffatt, Matt Everitt, Lucrezia Millarini and Stephen K Amos are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of The Sitcom Bottom, Glastonbury Festival, Die Hard and Fela Kuti

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: In the Lothians and the East of Scotland, the judges visit a glass-fronted home, a country cottage, and a stunning Georgian townhouse

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Ruby excitedly plans an extra-special welcome home lunch for Caitlin who is being discharged from hospital, while Eve grows suspicious of Cameron when Stevie unwittingly unravels the lies he has told her. Alex's search for Lenny highlights a new suspect

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four of the most memorable professional chefs from past series - Steve Barringer, Exose Grant Lopo-Ndigna, Oli Martin and Olivia Burt - are invited to return to the MasterChef kitchen to compete in a festive-themed special. First, the chefs have the chance to relive the much-feared Skills Test, and catch up with judges on how their careers have progressed since taking part in the competition. Later, they are tasked to create a seasonally themed two-course menu in two-and-half hours to impress the judges

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy plan some New Year's Eve festivities in this Christmas special, with both inviting their parents to join them. Things soon go badly wrong as party games give way to an uncompromising round of guests pointing out what New Year's resolutions everyone needs to make - with a distinct lack of festive spirit on display. Starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Pandemonium
programme.desc: One-off video diary comedy, in which a boy documents his family's disastrous year. With their summer holiday to California having been cancelled and their finances devastated due to the pandemic, family morale is at an all-time low. But the Jessops make one final stab at trying to salvage something from 2020 with a trip to Margate in the middle of December

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents action from the final Premiership matches of 2020, including Celtic v Dundee United, St Mirren v Rangers and Motherwell v Kilmarnock

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Newcastle United v Liverpool at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Fulham at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Reds and Spurs will have expected to both claim three points to aid their respective title-challenges. Plus, a round-up of Monday and Tuesday's goals

programme.title: Highlander (1986)
programme.desc: A 16th-century Scottish clansman discovers he is one of a race of immortal warriors who can only be killed by beheading, and who are destined to fight to the death until just one survives. Centuries later in modern-day New York, he prepares for a final showdown with his deadliest enemy. Fantasy action adventure, starring Christopher Lambert, Sean Connery, Clancy Brown and Roxanne Hart. Featuring a soundtrack by Queen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Home (2015)
programme.desc: An alien on the run from his own people arrives on Earth and embarks on an adventure with a daring young woman. It turns out to be a life-changing trip during which they begin to understand the true meaning of the word home. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin and Jennifer Lopez

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Grandpa's Great Escape
programme.desc: Jack's grandfather was a Second World War flying ace who now sadly has Alzheimer's disease, and when his family can no longer look after him he is moved to Twilight Towers care home, run by matron Miss Dandy. It soon becomes clear she is running the place for her own ulterior motives and it is up to Jack to help his grandpa make a daring escape and once again take to the skies in a Spitfire. Adaptation of David Walliams' children's book, starring Tom Courtenay, Kit Connor and Jennifer Saunders

programme.title: The Highway Rat
programme.desc: A ravenous rat craves buns, biscuits and all sweet things and as he tears along the highway, he is on the lookout for sugary treats to steal, but his sweet tooth leads him toward a sticky end. Rob Brydon narrates the animated comedy based on the book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler, with the voices of David Tennant, Nina Sosanya, Tom Hollander and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Stick Man
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It follows the title character on an incredible adventure across the seasons as he runs from a playful dog, gets thrown into a river, escapes from a swan's nest and even ends up on top of a fire. Will he ever get back to his family in time for Christmas? Martin Freeman heads the voice cast as Stick Man, with Hugh Bonneville, Russell Tovey, Rob Brydon and Sally Hawkins, plus Jennifer Saunders as the narrator

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (2017)
programme.desc: A prince is transformed into a hideous monster as punishment for his selfishness, and can only regain his human form by finding true love. Salvation comes in the form of a spirited village girl, who offers to take her father's place when he is imprisoned in the monster's castle. But as romance blossoms between the unlikely pair, a jealous rival suitor only sees a beast to be slain. Live action remake of the Disney animated fairytale, starring Emma Watson, Dan Stevens and Luke Evans, with Ewan McGregor, Emma Thompson and Ian McKellen playing the Beast's entourage of living ornaments

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo present some of the best music of the year, featuring Alfie Templeman, Arlo Parks, Becky Hill, DJ Nathan Dawe featuring KSI and Ella Henderson, Sigala and James Arthur, Yungblud, and Wes Nelson featuring Hardy Caprio. They also relive performances by Alicia Keys, Michael Kiwanuka and more

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Paralympians Hannah Cockcroft and Nathan Maguire, father-and-son presenting duo Martin and Roman Kemp and singer Fleur East and her blogger sister Keshia show off their music knowledge by guessing the songs after hearing a few seconds of each track. The winning pair get the chance to win up to £10,000 for a charity of their choice - but the longer they take, the more they lose from the jackpot. Hosted by Marvin and Rochelle Humes

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the residents of Walford prepare to welcome in the new year, things are not well in the Carter household. Consumed with emotions about his past, Mick finds himself struggling to cope with the enormity of the situation. As he battles with past demons, he begins to crumble

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this second special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker and actress Sally Lindsay teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Hogmanay 2020
programme.desc: Susan Calman gets ready for Hogmanay 2020 with a studio of stars looking ahead to celebrating The Bells as the midnight moment approaches

programme.title: The Big New Year's In
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness kicks off the countdown to 2021 with a star-studded live extravaganza promising fun, music and games. Viewers are also invited to play along with a hilarious quiz looking back at an extraordinary 12 months

programme.title: Only an Excuse?
programme.desc: Jonathan Watson leads a hilarious mix of classic sketches, popular characters, famous faces and up-to-date skits tackling 2020 from a footballing perspective. Featuring nostalgic recollections from guest stars, plus fresh material covering Scottish football and the world in general

programme.title: Hogmanay 2020
programme.desc: Susan Calman and her guests welcome in the new year, with music by Deacon Blue, Amy Macdonald, the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Blazin' Fiddles joining Hannah Rarity for Auld Lang Syne. Amy Irons reports live as the traditional piper high on the ramparts of Edinburgh Castle marks the midnight moment

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Festive episode of the popular Scottish comedy, in which Jack and Victor face the prospect of a gloomy Hogmanay stuck in the lift at Osprey Heights, and console themselves by recalling New Years past. As the clock ticks down to 2007, Winston is also in a reminiscent mood, and takes refuge in memories of a legendary holiday season when Craigland really knew how to throw a party. Ford Kiernan, Greg Hemphill and Paul Riley star

Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford and Brighton & Hove Albion v Arsenal at Amex Stadium. United and Wolves met on four occasions in all competitions last term, with three of those contests ending level and the Red Devils prevailing 1-0 at home in an FA Cup replay in the other. Brighton did the double over the Gunners last season, winning 2-1 home and away, and will be hopeful of shocking the Londoners once again. Plus, a round-up of the rest of tonight and yesterday's fixtures

programme.title: Point Break (1991)
programme.desc: An FBI agent infiltrates a Californian surfing gang suspected of carrying out a series of armed robberies wearing masks modelled on former US presidents. However, his loyalties are tested to the limits when he is befriended by the gang's leader. Kathryn Bigelow's thriller, starring Keanu Reeves, Patrick Swayze, Gary Busey and Lori Petty

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The annual harvest festival brings the whole village together, but one resident's announcement causes unexpected emotional consequences for the community. Spoof documentary exploring modern life in the Cotswolds, written by and starring siblings Daisy May and Charlie Cooper

programme.title: Anita & Me (2002)
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Meena strikes up an unlikely friendship with blonde teenage tearaway Anita. Despite their ethnic and social differences, the girls manage to support each other through difficult times while their families cope with life's problems. Comedy drama set in a Midlands village in the 1970s and based on the novel by Meera Syal, starring Chandeep Uppal, Anna Brewster, Max Beesley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kathy Burke, Lynn Redgrave and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Strange Magic (2015)
programme.desc: A fairy living in an enchanted forest tries to rescue her sister from a monstrous creature. Meanwhile, the magical kingdom is thrown into chaos by a love potion that results in a series of unexpected romances. Animated fantasy musical, with the voices of Evan Rachel Wood, Alan Cumming and Kristin Chenoweth

programme.title: Early Man (2018)
programme.desc: A caveman tribe must learn to play football in order to regain their homeland from a conquering civilisation. Animation, with the voice of Eddie Redmayne

programme.title: Gangsta Granny
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Schoolboy Ben is bored witless after being made to stay with his grandma. All she ever wants to do is play Scrabble and eat yucky cabbage soup. But it turns out there's a whole lot more to this OAP than cardigans and toffees when she tells him how she used to be an international jewel thief - and when she admits there was one heist she never quite managed in her heyday, the pair set out on a criminal adventure. Julia McKenzie stars as the shady senior citizen, with Walliams and Miranda Hart as the boys' ballroom-dancing parents, alongside Joanna Lumley, Rob Brydon and Robbie Williams, with Reece Buttery as Ben

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Room on the Broom
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It tells the story of a kind witch who invites a surprising number of animals to ride on her broom - much to the frustration of her cat. But when a fearsome dragon appears, what will the gang do? Gillian Anderson heads the voice cast as the witch, with Rob Brydon, Martin Clunes, Sally Hawkins and David Walliams as the animals, Timothy Spall as the dragon and Simon Pegg as narrator

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake

programme.title: Dragons: Gift of the Night Fury
programme.desc: Animated short film featuring the characters from How to Train Your Dragon, in which the Vikings prepare to celebrate their winter festival, only to find all the winged reptiles have mysteriously disappeared. Hiccup tries to discover where they have gone, and stumbles on a mysterious island. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Young Viking Hiccup has successfully persuaded his people to live in peace with dragons, but now faces a new threat from a warlord who is capturing the flying lizards to build an army. As Hiccup tries to keep the peace, he is reunited with his long-lost mother. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Jay Baruchel, Gerard Butler and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host another sport-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Famous faces Scarlett Moffatt, Matt Everitt, Lucrezia Millarini and Stephen K Amos are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of The Sitcom Bottom, Glastonbury Festival, Die Hard and Fela Kuti

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Among the items coming into the barn for a spruce-up include an iconic but dilapidated Chopper bike which once lit up Christmas morning for two brothers. A much-loved but threadbare teddy bear in need of a stitch in time provides another challenge, along with a broken-down clockwork train set and a treasured harmonium which has been silent for decades. Jay Blades presents

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four of the most memorable professional chefs from past series - Steve Barringer, Exose Grant Lopo-Ndigna, Oli Martin and Olivia Burt - are invited to return to the MasterChef kitchen to compete in a festive-themed special. First, the chefs have the chance to relive the much-feared Skills Test, and catch up with judges on how their careers have progressed since taking part in the competition. Later, they are tasked to create a seasonally themed two-course menu in two-and-half hours to impress the judges

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy plan some New Year's Eve festivities in this Christmas special, with both inviting their parents to join them. Things soon go badly wrong as party games give way to an uncompromising round of guests pointing out what New Year's resolutions everyone needs to make - with a distinct lack of festive spirit on display. Starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Pandemonium
programme.desc: One-off video diary comedy, in which a boy documents his family's disastrous year. With their summer holiday to California having been cancelled and their finances devastated due to the pandemic, family morale is at an all-time low. But the Jessops make one final stab at trying to salvage something from 2020 with a trip to Margate in the middle of December

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Newcastle United v Liverpool at St James' Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Fulham at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Reds and Spurs will have expected to both claim three points to aid their respective title-challenges. Plus, a round-up of Monday and Tuesday's goals

programme.title: Highlander (1986)
programme.desc: A 16th-century Scottish clansman discovers he is one of a race of immortal warriors who can only be killed by beheading, and who are destined to fight to the death until just one survives. Centuries later in modern-day New York, he prepares for a final showdown with his deadliest enemy. Fantasy action adventure, starring Christopher Lambert, Sean Connery, Clancy Brown and Roxanne Hart. Featuring a soundtrack by Queen

programme.title: Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa (2013)
programme.desc: The hapless local radio DJ ends up in the centre of a crisis when a colleague gets fired by the station's new owners, only to respond by returning to the studio with a shotgun and taking hostages. The police enlist Alan's aid as a negotiator, giving him the chance to revive his own flagging public profile. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Colm Meaney and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Home (2015)
programme.desc: An alien on the run from his own people arrives on Earth and embarks on an adventure with a daring young woman. It turns out to be a life-changing trip during which they begin to understand the true meaning of the word home. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin and Jennifer Lopez

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens (2009)
programme.desc: A woman is exposed to radiation from a meteorite that causes her to grow to an enormous size during her wedding. She is captured by the military and held prisoner with an assortment of strange creatures - but when aliens invade, these freaks of nature are the only ones capable of saving the world. Animated sci-fi comedy, with the voices of Reese Witherspoon and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Grandpa's Great Escape
programme.desc: Jack's grandfather was a Second World War flying ace who now sadly has Alzheimer's disease, and when his family can no longer look after him he is moved to Twilight Towers care home, run by matron Miss Dandy. It soon becomes clear she is running the place for her own ulterior motives and it is up to Jack to help his grandpa make a daring escape and once again take to the skies in a Spitfire. Adaptation of David Walliams' children's book, starring Tom Courtenay, Kit Connor and Jennifer Saunders

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Highway Rat
programme.desc: A ravenous rat craves buns, biscuits and all sweet things and as he tears along the highway, he is on the lookout for sugary treats to steal, but his sweet tooth leads him toward a sticky end. Rob Brydon narrates the animated comedy based on the book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler, with the voices of David Tennant, Nina Sosanya, Tom Hollander and Frances de la Tour

programme.title: Stick Man
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It follows the title character on an incredible adventure across the seasons as he runs from a playful dog, gets thrown into a river, escapes from a swan's nest and even ends up on top of a fire. Will he ever get back to his family in time for Christmas? Martin Freeman heads the voice cast as Stick Man, with Hugh Bonneville, Russell Tovey, Rob Brydon and Sally Hawkins, plus Jennifer Saunders as the narrator

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (2017)
programme.desc: A prince is transformed into a hideous monster as punishment for his selfishness, and can only regain his human form by finding true love. Salvation comes in the form of a spirited village girl, who offers to take her father's place when he is imprisoned in the monster's castle. But as romance blossoms between the unlikely pair, a jealous rival suitor only sees a beast to be slain. Live action remake of the Disney animated fairytale, starring Emma Watson, Dan Stevens and Luke Evans, with Ewan McGregor, Emma Thompson and Ian McKellen playing the Beast's entourage of living ornaments

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo present some of the best music of the year, featuring Alfie Templeman, Arlo Parks, Becky Hill, DJ Nathan Dawe featuring KSI and Ella Henderson, Sigala and James Arthur, Yungblud, and Wes Nelson featuring Hardy Caprio. They also relive performances by Alicia Keys, Michael Kiwanuka and more

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Paralympians Hannah Cockcroft and Nathan Maguire, father-and-son presenting duo Martin and Roman Kemp and singer Fleur East and her blogger sister Keshia show off their music knowledge by guessing the songs after hearing a few seconds of each track. The winning pair get the chance to win up to £10,000 for a charity of their choice - but the longer they take, the more they lose from the jackpot. Hosted by Marvin and Rochelle Humes

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this second special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker and actress Sally Lindsay teaming up to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As the residents of Walford prepare to welcome in the new year, things are not well in the Carter household. Consumed with emotions about his past, Mick finds himself struggling to cope with the enormity of the situation. As he battles with past demons, he begins to crumble

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The second of two festive shows sees The Vivienne, Lesley Joseph, Sabrina Grant and Sally Phillips competing to show off their dress-making skills. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a child's party skirt and then turn old clothes into a recognisable pantomime character in the transformation challenge. They then create a made-to-measure party dress that evokes memories of a particular New Year's Eve - with surprising results

programme.title: The Big New Year's In
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness kicks off the countdown to 2021 with a star-studded live extravaganza promising fun, music and games. Viewers are also invited to play along with a hilarious quiz looking back at an extraordinary 12 months

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham has a star-studded line-up for his final show of the year, including Hollywood everyman Tom Hanks, talking about his new Western News of the World, and Emily Blunt and Jamie Dornan, who have teamed up in the romantic drama Wild Mountain Thyme - a film shoe trailer has already caused a stir on the internet due to the actors' questionable Irish accents. Jessica Chastain gives the lowdown on her new spy thriller The 355, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall has advice on better eating and Nish Kumar gives his satirical take on the past 12 months. Rounding off the show, Sophie Ellis-Bextor - whose online kitchen disco shows proved a hit this summer - performs Crying at the Discotheque

programme.title: Alicia Keys Rocks New Year's Eve
programme.desc: The multi-award-winning singer-songwriter Alicia Keys takes to the stage to perform many of her hits and plenty more besides in this special concert, recorded on location in Los Angeles and building up to the imminent arrival of 2021

programme.title: Happy New Year Live!
programme.desc: The chimes of Big Ben, followed by this year's alternative to the annual London fireworks, which have been cancelled due to the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: Alicia Keys Rocks New Year's Eve
programme.desc: The celebrations continue as Alicia and her band perform more of her greatest hits to welcome in the new year

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: Festive episode of the popular Scottish comedy, in which Jack and Victor face the prospect of a gloomy Hogmanay stuck in the lift at Osprey Heights, and console themselves by recalling New Years past. As the clock ticks down to 2007, Winston is also in a reminiscent mood, and takes refuge in memories of a legendary holiday season when Craigland really knew how to throw a party. Ford Kiernan, Greg Hemphill and Paul Riley star

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement to the House of Commons by the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak, on this year's spending review and the latest Office for Budget Responsibility reports with the responses from Shadow Chancellor Anneliese Dodds and SNP Treasury spokesperson, Alison Thewliss, from earlier today

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of the last question session of 2020 in the House of Lords

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport select committee session on the economics of music streaming with evidence from Colin Young, Tom Gray, Tom Frederikse, Guy Garvey, Ed O'Brien, Nadine Shah, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Lt General HR McMaster who served as Mr Trump's national security adviser until he was fired in 2018. Will the Trump years be seen as an aberration or a marker of change?

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Coverage of a debate on the future of football in Westminster Hall, from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Dr Chris Whitty, the Chief Medical Officer for England, delivering a lecture at Gresham College on 'the Geography of Ill Health', from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint session of the Health and Social Care and Science and Technology committees. MPs will hear from Prof Devi Sridhar, Professor of Global Public Health at the University of Edinburgh, and Health and Social Care Secretary Matt Hancock, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, chief exploration scientist at Nasa

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Lords on the Integrated Review of Security, Defence, Development and Foreign Policy, from earlier today

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate from the chamber

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: C-SPAN's coverage of the Senate Judiciary committee hearing on the role of tech giants during the presidential election campaign with Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey, from Tuesday 17 November.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Westminster Hall - Funding for Transport for London Coverage of a debate in Westminster Hall on funding for Transport for London, from Monday 23 November

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In a special edition of the programme, HARDtalk is in northwest England, to the area known as the Lake District. The landscape is beautiful but is not wild. The fields have been shaped by generations of shepherds and stockmen. Stephen Sackur speaks to James Rebanks whose farm has been in his family's hands for at least 600 years. He has become a nationally renowned advocate for a better kind of farming that is more sustainable and environmentally responsible. But are his ideas compatible with putting affordable food on all of our tables?

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions to the Executive Office in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The short debate in the House of Commons marking the 60th anniversary of Coronation Street, opened by the MP and former Coronation Street actor, Tracy Brabin, from Wednesday 2 December

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament
programme.desc: Conservative-led debate in the Welsh Parliament on the impact of Covid on health services, from Wednesday 25 November. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 28 November

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of the day's proceedings in the House of Commons including backbench business debates on the regulation and prevention of online harms and on International Men's Day followed by an adjournment debate on the Department for Health and Social Care's Independent Reconfiguration Panel.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: As soon as he emerged from his coma Alexei Navalny, the Russian opposition leader apparently poisoned by novichok nerve agent, expressed his determination to return to Moscow. But what future is there for an anti-Putin political movement in a country where dissent is all too often seriously bad for your health? Stephen Sackur speaks to Leonid Volkov, opposition politician and chief of staff to Mr Navalny. Is there any weakening of the Kremlin's grip on power?

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Peers quiz government minister Lord Parkinson on plans for an additional bank holiday to mark the Queen's Platinum Jubilee in 2022, from Monday 16 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland Affairs select committee session on cross-border cooperation on policing, security and criminal justice with evidence from the Police Service of Northern Ireland, the National Crime Agency and Her Majesty's Customs and Revenue, from Wednesday 4 November.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the point of the world's nuclear watchdog the International Atomic Energy Agency? Its task is to ensure that countries intent on developing nuclear power don't use their programmes as cover for development of weapons of mass destruction. But is the task impossible? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the new IAEA chief, Rafael Grossi. From the continued bitter arguments over Iran, to North Korea, and Saudi Arabia, is the IAEA another example of a global agency undermined by geopolitical division?

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the International Trade Secretary Liz Truss and her team of ministers.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: The statement in the Scottish Parliament by the First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon, on the Covid-19 pandemic and the possible need to strengthen the top level of Covid-19 restrictions in Scotland as the whole of the Scottish mainland moves to level 4 from Boxing Day, from Tuesday 22 December

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Interviews with newsmakers and personalities from across the globe

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the joint session of the Science and Technology committee and the Health and Social Care committee looking at the performance of NHS Test and Trace with evidence from the Executive Chair, Dido Harding, and its Chief Medical Adviser, Dr Susan Hopkins.

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's programme looking back at the final full week in Westminster of 2020. First shown on BBC Parliament on Thursday 17 December

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: The news conference held in Downing Street by the Prime Minister, Boris Johnson, on the announcement of a Tier 4 for London and many areas of the South East as well as changes to the restrictions over Christmas, alongside the government's chief scientific adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance, and the Chief Medical Officer for England, Professor Chris Whitty, from Saturday 19 December.

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer giving a speech on devolution across the UK and moving power out of London, from Monday 21 December

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Director of the Victoria and Albert Museum, Tristram Hunt, delivering this year's Orwell Memorial Lecture on 'Decolonising The Wonder House: Orwell, Empire and the Museum', from Monday 16 November.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement to the House of Commons by the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak, on this year's spending review and the latest Office for Budget Responsibility reports with the responses from Shadow Chancellor Anneliese Dodds and SNP Treasury spokesperson, Alison Thewliss, from earlier today

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament
programme.desc: Conservative-led debate in the Welsh Parliament on the impact of Covid on health services, from Wednesday 25 November. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 28 November

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of the day's proceedings in the House of Commons including backbench business debates on the regulation and prevention of online harms and on International Men's Day followed by an adjournment debate on the Department for Health and Social Care's Independent Reconfiguration Panel.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: As soon as he emerged from his coma Alexei Navalny, the Russian opposition leader apparently poisoned by novichok nerve agent, expressed his determination to return to Moscow. But what future is there for an anti-Putin political movement in a country where dissent is all too often seriously bad for your health? Stephen Sackur speaks to Leonid Volkov, opposition politician and chief of staff to Mr Navalny. Is there any weakening of the Kremlin's grip on power?

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Peers quiz government minister Lord Parkinson on plans for an additional bank holiday to mark the Queen's Platinum Jubilee in 2022, from Monday 16 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland Affairs select committee session on cross-border cooperation on policing, security and criminal justice with evidence from the Police Service of Northern Ireland, the National Crime Agency and Her Majesty's Customs and Revenue, from Wednesday 4 November.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the point of the world's nuclear watchdog the International Atomic Energy Agency? Its task is to ensure that countries intent on developing nuclear power don't use their programmes as cover for development of weapons of mass destruction. But is the task impossible? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the new IAEA chief, Rafael Grossi. From the continued bitter arguments over Iran, to North Korea, and Saudi Arabia, is the IAEA another example of a global agency undermined by geopolitical division?

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the International Trade Secretary Liz Truss and her team of ministers.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: 100 Women
programme.desc: For thousands of years, the Inuit people lived off the land. Nomadic hunters, chasing targets depending on the seasons. Now those weather patterns have utterly and irreversibly shifted. Locals say that sea ice used to form in September. Now, it forms in October or as late as November. The Arctic is now warming at least twice as fast as the rest of the globe, according to the Arctic Program Report Card. Climate change is threatening the Inuits’ survival - both physically and mentally. Inuit women are trying to hold their families together through it all. Life on Thin Ice follows three generations of Inuit women; a grandmother, mother and daughter to see the impact that climate change has had on their community. What else have they lost along with the ice?

programme.title: 100 Women Extra
programme.desc: Finland has a coalition government led by five women - four of them are in their 30s. Their prime minister, Sanna Marin, was the youngest world leader when she was elected. Finland's coalition government has been praised for their quick and decisive action on the coronavirus pandemic, but a coalition can also mean compromise. The BBC has had world-first exclusive access behind the scenes to explore how the coalition works in practice, how representative it is of Finland's society, and what impact the age and sex of the government's main players has had on its policies and effectiveness.

programme.desc: For almost 40 years, DK MacPhee has lived with dystonia, which leaves him unable to straighten his head. DK gives an insight into the impact this has on his daily life and his ongoing battle to find treatment or relief. As his condition has deteriorated, with his head falling further to one side, the more his life has been affected. A number of years ago, he had to give up playing football, weekly, with his friends, something which he very much misses and he is determined to try and play again. Will this be possible?

programme.title: NY2SY
programme.desc: In a focused and candid delivery, Niall Iain Macdonald recounts his adventure story, attempting to conquer the might of the Atlantic Ocean in a solo rowing boat. Supported by footage taken over several years, both ashore and at sea, he shares the dramatic highs and the extreme lows of his 2018 adventure. In the lead-up to his latest attempt he records his slow, steady progress, the cancellations, postponements, accidents and frustrations that met him along the way, and also the sheer elation of finally setting out to sea. Illustrated with unique footage captured on board as he rowed
