programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How Japan's early victories over Allied forces during the Second World War convinced its people that their strength and discipline would bring them victory. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Allied offensive in the Pacific, where America's two-pronged attack led to heavy casualties on both sides, as the Japanese mounted a ferocious defence. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Enola Gay drops the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima in August 1945 as America bids to secure an unconditional surrender from the Japanese, even though conventional bombing raids had already flattened the country. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Holocaust Escape Tunnel
programme.desc: Archaeologists search for a rumoured escape route from a Nazi execution site in Poland that led to a nearby Synagogue, which was later destroyed by the Soviet army

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The award-winning documentary series reveals how the end of the Second World War resulted in Britain's military and economic exhaustion, leaving Europe at the mercy of sparring superpowers as the Cold War era dawned. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs' classic documentary explores the Second World War's powerful legacy. For many people the conflict was the most significant experience of their lives, but it also left millions dead - ordinary civilians and victims of persecution, as well as military casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an exploration of Earth's vast seas, which cover 70 per cent of the planet's surface. In the first episode, millions of squid appear off the coast of California, gannets, whale sharks and dolphins hunt migrating sardines in the Indian Ocean, and thousands of turtles make the annual pilgrimage to lay their eggs in Costa Rica

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Camera crews journey to the deepest parts of the world's oceans to explore the lives of their inhabitants, including fearsome predators with huge teeth, strange jellyfish and deep-sea sharks that lurk among the gigantic submerged mountains and volcanos. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Predators and prey in the open oceans come under the scrutiny of David Attenborough. Marlin track down a shoal of sardines as a sei whale scoops up the leftovers, dolphins herd mackerel and attract the attention of a sailfish with the resonance of their sonar, and giant sunfish queue up to allow other species, including seagulls, to feed on their parasites

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Marine life at the poles, where winter temperatures frequently fall to a numbing -40C. Even when the sun reappears in the spring, its weak rays take a long time to melt the ice. David Attenborough describes how wildlife including seals, whales, polar bears and penguins overcome the elements to survive through prolonged cold spells

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A formerly luxurious and strategic mountain-top villa on the island of Rhodes in the Mediterranean that's been ravaged by acts of revenge by locals since 1955

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues the final leg of his journey, meeting a survivor of the Coventry Blitz and exploring how the city has been rebuilt since the 1940 bombardment. Reaching Aylesbury in Buckinghamshire, he stops overnight at Hartwell House and visits the area's last pure-breed duck farmer, before heading to Watford in Hertfordshire to learn about Operation Pied Piper

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches London, where he continues to discover what remains of George Bradshaw's Britain. Arriving at St Pancras, he learns about the station's troubled history and £800million makeover, before travelling along the world's first underground line from King's Cross to Farringdon. The trip comes to an end at the Houses of Parliament, where he climbs the clock tower and meets the men responsible for keeping Big Ben working

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek takes a shine to a rare 1958 Armstrong Siddeley Sapphire with a long family history and a convertible Triumph Herald brings a few smiles on auction day

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A strange-looking town with a supernatural atmosphere, a collection of imposing buildings in southern Australia, and the remains of curious-looking basins emerging from the waves

programme.title: Underground Worlds

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. The first episode features the accounts of Polish inmates held there prior to the Holocaust, Jewish survivors and a former SS officer, who discusses being placed in charge of a firing squad

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at the early stages of genocide at the camp, examining the role of commander Rudolf Hoess in the process. Despite later claiming he was only following orders, Hoess was instrumental in the development of Auschwitz for mass murder, arranging for Soviet prisoners to be brought in as construction workers and scouring Europe for more victims to kill. Featuring contributions from survivors

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: An exploration of the world's most densely inhabited waters, where variations in temperature owing to the year-long cycle of the sun encourage a vast array of marine life, from seals and sharks in the winter, to lobster and salmon in the summer, dolphins in the autumn and the walking handfish in spring. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Veteran naturalist David Attenborough explores the fragile marine ecosystem of the coral reef, where all creatures, including the corals themselves, have to fight for survival. From tiny beginnings, the reef grows and develops over hundreds of years, providing support for innumerable animals - yet one storm can threaten the entire community

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates the opportunities tides create for marine life, showing giant stingrays gliding on currents for a rest, South African sea snails surfing the waves in pursuit of a meal, crabs playing football on a beach, and bottlenose dolphins embarking on a dangerous game of catch. In the oceanic world each turn of the tide can mean life or death for its inhabitants

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Exploring the wealth of marine life that inhabits the ocean's edge, dealing with the dangers of an ever-shifting environment. David Attenborough observes nesting seabirds in east Russia, Patagonian killer whales, Newfoundland caplin fish, walruses, and Brazilian green turtles - which make their annual expedition to an island in the middle of the Atlantic to lay their eggs

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Ruins from the time before the fall of the Berlin Wall, including a covert Communist base that hid Cold War submarines for Tito and the former HQ of the Soviet Army in East Germany

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the nation with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and find out what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins by exploring Brighton's Victorian aquarium, the underground quarries of Godstone, Surrey, and the wonders of the Crystal Palace in suburban south-east London

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey, learning about the London Necropolis Railway, a funeral service that transported coffins from Waterloo to Surrey's Brookwood Cemetery from 1854 to 1941. He also discovers how London's West End became a popular shopping destination in the 19th century and examines the changing fortunes of the city's docks

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up some 'emerging' sporting classics from the Far East when three Datsun Zs appear, and an unusual lot arrives that run on caterpillar tracks not wheels

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits the former HQ of London Underground, 55 Broadway, which was the capital's first skyscraper, and sees the timber-framed Barmouth Bridge in north Wales

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A closer look at a sprawling Wild West ghost town that sits peaceful and abandoned in Montana, USA, but is still haunted by tales of murder, corruption and buried treasure

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: The secrets of the largest subterranean fortress along France's border with Germany, and a look at how a unique precious mineral was discovered in Derbyshire

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Stratolaunch is designed to lift rockets, spacecraft and experimental high-speed hypersonic planes into the stratosphere, then release them for their onward journey into space

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Three Gorges Dam, built across China's Yangtze river, which is the largest and heaviest concrete structure ever built, housing the world's largest power station

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Millau Viaduct in South West France, which is the tallest bridge in the world and stretches two and a half kilometres over one of Europe's deepest valleys

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The history of the concentration camp, charting its origins as a place to hold political prisoners through its development into a mechanised factory for mass murder. The first episode features the accounts of Polish inmates held there prior to the Holocaust, Jewish survivors and a former SS officer, who discusses being placed in charge of a firing squad

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at the early stages of genocide at the camp, examining the role of commander Rudolf Hoess in the process. Despite later claiming he was only following orders, Hoess was instrumental in the development of Auschwitz for mass murder, arranging for Soviet prisoners to be brought in as construction workers and scouring Europe for more victims to kill. Featuring contributions from survivors

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at how corruption became rife within the SS, with the money stolen from Jews finding its way into the pockets of the camp guards rather than funding the war effort. Many of the SS also enjoyed illicit sexual relationships with female prisoners. Meanwhile, Dr Mengele embarked on a gruesome series of experiments, using children as guinea pigs in his research into genetic inheritance

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The events of 1944, when the death toll rose substantially. By this point, the Allies were aware of Auschwitz's purpose, and faced a difficult decision over what should be done about it. Plus, the story of the occupation of Hungary, revealing how Adolf Eichmann offered to spare a million Jews in return for certain services

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The final episode examines the liberation of Auschwitz and other camps by the Soviet Red Army. However, freedom did not mean an end to the prisoners' suffering - many were mistreated by their liberators and thousands died for the want of medical treatment

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of marine life in different environments around the north, beginning with a tropical coral reef which has medicinal properties for dolphins, and is used as a tool by tusk fish. In Japan, a shipwreck is home to the Asian sheepshead wrasse, which can change gender, while in the polar north, walruses struggle to find ice floes for their pups to rest on as climate change takes its toll on the environment

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live in volcanic hot spots on the ocean bed, areas that reach temperatures hot enough to melt lead and which may hold the secrets behind all life on Earth. Other strange creatures from the depths of the sea include bone-eating worms, fish that walk rather than swim, and hordes of squid. Plus, footage of sharks in a feeding frenzy over the dead body of a whale and the wildlife of the Midnight Zone in the Pacific, an area that never receives any natural light, forcing creatures to rely on their own bioluminescence

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Creatures that live on coral reefs, including groupers, small predators that scientists believe are as intelligent as chimpanzees. These creatures have a remarkable aptitude for communication to the point where they can interact with other species including octopi and work together to catch prey. Plus, footage of a vast spawning event involving several different species of fish, coral and invertebrates, and time-lapse photography of coral polyps growing and dying, creating a structure visible from space

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Majid Ravandi hosts the final dinner party in south London, but fails to impress with a hot prawn starter and a dry main course. As the meal progresses, a bickering Joe and Karen threaten to spoil the cook's friendly demeanour, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a Gloucestershire couple find a holiday home in Fuerteventura that is large enough to accommodate three generations of their family

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton searches for a retirement home on Spain's Costa de Almeria for Ted and Diane Brewer, who met on holiday 20 years ago and would now like to live in the sun

programme.title: Snackmasters
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix pits a leading chocolatier against a virtuoso pastry chef to replicate one of the nation's best-loved festive chocolate brands - Quality Street. As ever, the chefs must work out the secret techniques and recipes behind the household favourites before creating their own versions to be judged by a panel of Quality Street insiders and experts. But with so many to choose from, will they be expected to remake the green triangle, the orange crunch or the iconic purple one?

programme.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles (1987)
programme.desc: A stressed businessman just wants to make it home to spend Thanksgiving with his family. However, when his flight is forced to land miles from its destination, he finds himself sharing a disaster-strewn journey across America with an amiable but slobbish salesman. Comedy road movie, starring Steve Martin, John Candy, Laila Robins, Kevin Bacon and Michael McKean

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Opinionated viewers the Siddiquis, Giles and Mary, Jenny and Lee, Ellie and Izzi, Mary and Marina, and Amira and Iqra look back on the year's biggest and best shows, including Strictly Come Dancing, Countdown, This Morning, Yorkshire Tea vs PG Tips: Battle of the Brews, Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat, CBeebies Bedtime Stories, The Undoing, First Dates, Borat Subsequent Moviefilm, Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On, Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?, Elephant, The Great British Bake Off, The Crown and Naked Attraction

programme.title: Back to the
programme.desc: The comedian examines Channel 4 in the 1990s, when its output included The Word, The Big Breakfast, Father Ted, Brass Eye and Drop the Dead Donkey, and viewers were introduced to future stars Ricky Gervais, Sacha Baron Cohen, Simon Pegg and Dermot O'Leary.

programme.title: Celebrity Snoop Dogs
programme.desc: A special festive edition of the show in which celebrities' dogs provide a unique perspective on their homes. The special twist this time is that celebrity dog owners Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Gok Wan and Joanna Page are watching on and competing against each other to throw the others off the scent as their four-legged friends roam around their lavishly decorated homes

programme.title: The Snowman and the Snowdog
programme.desc: Animated sequel to Raymond Briggs' classic festive tale The Snowman, telling the story of another youngster's magical Christmas. A boy's snowman and snowdog come to life at the stroke of midnight and take him on an adventure to the North Pole, where he and his new companions meet an assortment of colourful characters, including Santa himself, before returning home - where a wonderful surprise awaits

programme.title: The Tiger Who Came to Tea
programme.desc: Hand-drawn animated version of Judith Kerr's much-loved tale, which celebrated its 50th anniversary in 2018. A tea-guzzling tiger turns up unannounced and eats and drinks young Sophie and her mummy out of everything in the house, even draining the water from their taps. Narrated by David Walliams, with David Oyelowo providing the voice for the big cat alongside seven-year-old Clara Ross as Sophie, with other characters played by Benedict Cumberbatch, Tamsin Greig and Paul Whitehouse

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The contestants face two international challenges - a South African dessert that was a childhood favourite of Prue Leith and a decorated pie representing the nation of their choice

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Angie and Sandy from Nottingham are approaching retirement and looking for a place where they can spend quality time together. They've decided the sunshine of the southern Costa Blanca is the perfect place to do just that, so Ben Hillman shows them five properties for their £75,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps house hunters find their dream homes in popular winter sun destinations. Potential buyers explore the beautiful and serene island of Crete

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Farzana Kausar from Stockport, Greater Manchester, is looking for a better quality of life in the Spanish sun. Her daughter Aisha, who uses a wheelchair, is also making the full-time move. With a budget of £120,000 for the property and any alterations they'll need to make for Aisha, Laura Hamilton shows them five homes around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps house hunters find their dream homes in popular winter sun destinations. Potential buyers explore the beautiful La Cala de Mijas, Spain

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Barney borrows Homer's car and vanishes on a drunken binge. Weeks later, Homer gets a letter from New York saying the vehicle is illegally parked between the towers of the World Trade Centre. Although Homer hates the Big Apple - owing to an unfortunate incident from his youth - the family heads to there to try to get the car back

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A sleazy carnival worker and his son lose their jobs thanks to Homer, and end up moving in with the Simpson family - whose home they proceed to take over and completely wreck. With the guest voice of Jim Varney

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Mozz from Bury hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in and around Manchester, treating his guests to garlic stilton mushrooms and poached pears

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, voiceover artist and film extra Matt cooks up a Jewish Friday night supper, including his mum's traditional chicken soup

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Budding author and mum-of-five Cleona is next to host in Manchester, hoping a menu based on her Jamaican roots will get her guests onside

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Australian radio content director James hosts the fourth party in Manchester, hoping his menu containing Chinese cuisine and his mum's cake recipe can land him the top spot

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Events boss Sharon hosts the final night in Manchester. With her hearty decadent menu on offer, she has to win over Cleona after a bust up on from Cleona's turn as host. Sharon's skills and decorum are put to the test, though Mozz, Matt and James are on hand for moral support

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Marianne and Paul, who want a holiday home on a golf resort around the stunning Mar Menor in Murcia, Spain, on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: The Caribbean island of Nevis is very close to Steven and Murila Fifer's hearts. Murila was born there, and the couple have visited the place together many times over the years. They have now decided their dream holiday home should be on the island, so Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties within their £330,000 budget. Will they fall for a country villa or a beach-side home?

programme.title: The Italian Job (1969)
programme.desc: A crook assembles a motley gang to steal £4million in gold bullion from an Italian armoured car, which brings Turin to a standstill and enables the robbers to make a quick getaway in a fleet of Minis - but they end up falling foul of the Mafia along the way. Crime comedy, starring Michael Caine, Noel Coward, Benny Hill, Tony Beckley and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Sam Smith and Joe Wicks join Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty for an early New Year celebration. On the menu tonight are Italian-inspired pork and crispy double crackling, laid on a bed of veg and creamy beans and topped with a tangy herb salsa. Sam also contributes a childhood favourite food, blending the flavours of Mexico and Scotland - haggis tacos complete with home-made corn tortillas, red onion pickle, salsa and avocado, while Joe gets a masterclass in making fresh pasta

programme.title: Tom Allen Goes to Town
programme.desc: The comedian visits the West Yorkshire town of Wakefield, taking in one of the world's biggest rhubarb crops, exploring its beautiful cathedral and visiting one of the only drive-in fish and chip shops in the world. He also buys a round of Wakefield's famous steak pies and helps renovate the nearby Caribbean restaurant for the family he's been staying with. At the Theatre Royal, Tom is joined by a special assistant for a comedy show - none other than Spandau Ballet's Martin Kemp - entertaining host a full house of locals

programme.title: The Crystal Maze
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade guides husband and wife Spencer Matthews and Vogue Williams, Olympic cycling gold medallist Chris Hoy, reality TV star Jessica Wright and the Rev Richard Coles through the four zones of the Maze, tackling a range of skill, mystery, physical and mental challenges

programme.title: Back to the
programme.desc: The Big Brother presenter looks back on a decade of Channel 4 programmes, sharing a first-hand perspective on the dawn of the groundbreaking reality show. Davina reveals how shows from You Are What You Eat and How to Look Good Naked also reflected a fascination with the details of other people's lives, as well as celebrating dramas like Skins, Teachers and Sugar Rush and flagship Channel 4 shows like flagship favourites Come Dine with Me, Coach Trip and Embarrassing Bodies

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes with UK Sotheby's International Realty, with the coronavirus pandemic having seen a surge of super-rich buyers leaving towns and cities. The brokers set out to sell a property in Highgate, north London, that is on the market for £40 million, as well the home of former Liverpool FC CEO Peter Moore on the Wirral

programme.title: The Snowman
programme.desc: Raymond Briggs' festive favourite, a wordless animated fantasy telling the story of a small boy whose snowman magically comes to life and whisks him off on the journey of a lifetime to the North Pole. Howard Blake's score includes the award-winning song Walking in the Air, which was taken to number five in the charts by a young Aled Jones, but is here sung by Peter Auty

programme.title: Quentin Blake's Clown
programme.desc: Animation, based on the book by Quentin Blake, bringing to life the adventures of a little toy clown, thrown away with a load of old discarded items, as he goes on a journey to find a new loving home for himself and his friends. His journey takes him through the helter-skelter streets of the city, dodging scary dogs and even scarier adults. He even finds himself mistaken for a child and performs in a school Christmas assembly. However, each time Clown thinks he might just have found a perfect new owner, and his hopes start to rise, he gets thrown away again

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The young bakers' building skills are put to the test as they are challenged to make a cake that is intentionally the wrong way up

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps a couple find a holiday home on the Greek island of Crete, hoping to find a traditional village house on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton shows retired couple Angela and Bernie Ward five properties in Cyprus, hoping that on a budget of £185,000, one of them will prove to be their dream home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: After years of working hard to make ends meet and putting up with the British weather, Jamie and Jayne Pace have decided to make a better life for themselves. After falling in love with Cyprus on their honeymoon, there was only ever going to be one place they considered making that new start and Laura Hamilton shows them five potential homes for their budget of £55,000

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer takes Bart and Lisa to a rock festival featuring Sonic Youth, the Smashing Pumpkins and Cypress Hill, hoping to broaden their understanding of pop culture. Unfortunately, he ends up getting hired as a sideshow freak. Featuring the guest voices of Peter Frampton and Billy Corgan

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer acquires a pet monkey to help him with his chores, while Bart begins co-hosting a children's news programme with Lisa - and decides to take a leaf out of Kent Brockman's book, cynically concentrating on human interest stories in a bid to boost his own popularity

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ballroom dancing instructor Stuart hosts the first of five parties from in and around Newcastle, hoping his colourful but healthy menu will impress Adam, Harriet, Mo and Jude

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the second night of the competition from in and around Newcastle, and the turn of Adam to host. He opts for an evening of fine dining and up-market gastro pub food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nursing student Harriet hosts the third night of the competition in Newcastle, throwing a dinner party entirely themed around her equestrian hobby

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Day four in Newcastle sees videographer Jude hoping to win over her guests with a decidedly experimental vegetarian menu, which has a distinct lack of vegetables

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

There's all to play for as the other guests all believe they are in with a chance, except for veggie Jude who has realised that her disastrous menu has put paid to any chances of her grabbing the grand

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Robert and Sue Booth have fallen in love with Spain on holiday over the years and have now decided to take the plunge and buy a place of their own in the Mar Menor area of the Costa Calida. For their budget of £60,000 they are determined to get a sea view and enough space for Robert to barbecue to his heart's content. Presenter and property expert Jasmine Harman accepts the challenge of finding some options that fit the bill

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps a couple find a holiday home on the Greek island of Crete, hoping to find a traditional village house on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick, Angel, Dorothy and Arthur, as well as Angel's parents Jenni and Steve prepare to celebrate a family Christmas in France. With an old 19th-century chateau ledger found in the attic and classic cookbooks to hand, the Strawbridges take inspiration from Christmases past, discovering what food, festivities, gifts and games would have been like in bygone eras. Dick and Angel's imagination goes into overdrive as they create a winter wonderland with an ice rink at its heart. Later, as the family settles down to dinner, so the sound of traditional hand bells rings out across the chateau and they wonder if their exploration of the past will create chateau traditions for the future

programme.title: Back to the
programme.desc: Lenny Henry is the first of four celebrities taking viewers on a guided tour of all things Channel 4. Cameras also focus on cult live music strand The Tube, and hit shows Whose Line Is It Anyway, and The Comic Strip Presents

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In Coventry, the sheriffs chase £10,000 owed by a publican to a contractor. Meanwhile, agents Max and Steve head to central London to try to recover almost £8,000 owed by a former peer of the realm to a firm of accountants

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriffs Stewart and Elmor try to recover almost £2,400 from a company director for unpaid invoices in Manchester, but face a fierce reception from a family who deny the debtor lives or trades from the address. programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The dog unit embarks on a desperate manhunt amid fears that an armed and dangerous character is roaming the streets of Lincoln, while Craggy closes in on a suspected shoplifter with a bag full of surprises. programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon Moon and his dog Kanto are hot on the heels of some burglary suspects, while spot-checks by the unit on the A1 turn up a carrier bag full of cash. programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Barry Peach hunts for a man wanted for assault, officers are hot on the heels of a suspected drug dealer, a drunken driver tries every trick in the book to avoid giving a breath sample and dog-handler Jon Peacock has a late-night encounter with two unusual law-breakers

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. He focuses on the streets of London as he reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: McDonald's vs Burger King: Burger Wars
programme.desc: Documentary examining how the fast food giants grew from humble origins to become two of the most recognisable brands in the world. The programme assesses the chains' marketing ploys and promotional techniques, while vloggers and food critics compare the rivals' most notable products and the ways in which they are presented. Industry insiders also reveal the secrets behind both chains' success and how they see off the competition

programme.title: Inside Lidl at Christmas
programme.desc: With Britain facing its toughest December in memory, this programme goes behind the doors of the budget supermarket to find out how it is preparing for Christmas - and how it keeps its prices down. programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A woman is murdered while taking the guise of an FBI agent during a role-playing game, and her fellow participant, the son of a hedge fund billionaire, is kidnapped and held to ransom. His father explains the pair were estranged and he refuses to pay the abductors, and a bungled sting coupled with a lack of information about those responsible means Mac must turn to the forensic evidence to bring the man back alive. Crime drama, guest starring Megan Dodds (Not Going Out)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A crime-scene lab technician is murdered in a manner remarkably similar to how one of her predecessors died in 1957, and someone anonymously texts pictures of the victim to Mac and Jo. The investigators discover the woman had been out drinking with two colleagues on the night of her death and receive some help from the retired detective who investigated the original case. Guest starring Lee Majors (The Six Million Dollar Man) and Spencer Grammer (Greek)

programme.title: Mary Higgins Clark: Pretend You Don't See Her (2002)
programme.desc: A New York estate agent becomes the target of an assassin after she sees a murder take place. The police act quickly to ensure her safety, placing her in a witness protection programme that enables her to start a new life elsewhere. But increasingly sinister events force her to face the fact that her life might still be in danger. Thriller, starring Emma Samms, Beau Starr and Hannes Jaenicke

programme.title: Lucky Day (2002)
programme.desc: An aspiring actress is shocked when a friend is found dead just days after winning the lottery. Ignoring the warnings of police, she vows to get to the bottom of his murder herself. But her foolhardy inquisitiveness only serves to place her in peril as the trail leads to a familiar face. Thriller, adapted from a novel by Mary Higgins Clark, starring Amanda Donohoe, Gino Marrocco and Gregor Torzs

programme.title: Columbo: Caution - Murder Can Be Hazardous to Your Health (1991)
programme.desc: The host of a TV show is threatened by a rival, who has discovered evidence of his murky past. The presenter does away with his blackmailer using poisoned cigarettes, and seems to have pulled off the perfect crime - but a certain shabby sleuth is soon on the case and finds a few things that fail to add up. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and George Hamilton

programme.title: Columbo: A Trace of Murder (1997)
programme.desc: A conniving woman yearns to divorce her husband and set up home with her lover - but a pre-nuptial agreement means she stands to lose out. Instead, she devises a cunning plan to nullify the contract, framing her unsuspecting spouse for the murder of a business rival. However, she reckons without the shabby sleuth's unfailing ability to uncover the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Shera Danese, Barry Corbin and David Rasche

programme.title: Columbo Likes the Nightlife (2003)
programme.desc: The disheveled cop is drawn into the world of the Los Angeles club scene when he investigates the apparent suicide of a tabloid reporter, and uncovers evidence of a blackmail plot. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Matthew Rhys and Jennifer Sky

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate when a parole officer is killed in a hit-and-run. The detectives track down the last person to see her alive - an academic whose juvenile delinquent son becomes their prime suspect - but are also intrigued to learn the dead woman's family is travelling clan with a dislike of outsiders

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Fin investigate the case of a woman found raped and murdered underneath a flyover - and discover $1million of a new cancer drug in the boot of her car. Analysis reveals the dead woman's supplies were fake, linking her to a pharmaceutical racket - but another assault gives the detectives a more immediate concern. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Ice-T

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Dr Huang try to prove a convicted serial killer is responsible for the rape and murder of another woman, whose assailant was never identified. In doing so, they hope to give the victim's parents a sense of closure - but face a race against time as the man is due to be executed in three days. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, BD Wong and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man accused of the sexual abuse of his daughter is subsequently murdered, but laying the blame at the door of the chief suspects proves difficult. Several family members are in the frame, but despite evidence of a gun outside the victim's office, they appear to be innocent

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates the ritualistic murder of a boy, a woman and an elderly man, and finds they all figured in sexual abuse cases investigated by Benson. As more dead bodies turn up, it becomes clear the detective is the killer's next target - but the others find themselves unable to prove anything against the most likely suspect

programme.title: The Enemy Within
programme.desc: When an NSA analyst is killed in a car crash, Keaton and his team realise that she has stolen a piece of cloaking software that she intends to pass on to Tal. Shepherd decodes the encrypted message that Tal had left for the analyst, which reveals exactly where it was supposed to be delivered. Seizing their opportunity, Keaton asks Shepherd to go undercover, posing as the now dead analyst. Shepherd meets with Tal's contact, a young man called Braden. After a successful handover, the team learn that Tal is definitely in the US. In Washington DC, Tal meets with a mystery man who assures him that he can help with his FBI problem

programme.title: The Enemy Within
programme.desc: Flashbacks reveal events that led CIA Deputy Director Erica Shepherd to betray her country, and the investigation that put her on a collision course with Will Keaton

programme.title: Columbo: An Exercise in Fatality (1974)
programme.desc: The owner of a chain of health clubs kills a business partner who was about to expose his crooked dealings. Making the death look like an unfortunate weightlifting accident, he cunningly creates the perfect alibi using a tape recording of his victim's voice - but he reckons without the intervention of the shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Conrad and Philip Bruns

programme.title: Columbo: Rest in Peace, Mrs Columbo (1990)
programme.desc: The wife of a criminal who died in prison swears revenge on the men she holds responsible for his incarceration - including Columbo. She kills one of her targets, and the shabby sleuth is assigned to the case. As she faces his well-honed approach to sniffing out a suspect, she plots to murder his wife. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Helen Shaver and Ian McShane

programme.title: Columbo: Agenda for Murder (1990)
programme.desc: A crooked lawyer hopes to start a promising political career, but a former client he saved from prison threatens to blow the whistle on him. The attorney murders the blackmailer and makes it look like suicide - but the shabby sleuth sees through his ruse and sets out to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Louis Zorich and Patrick McGoohan, who also directed

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Part one of two. Detectives Goren and Eames investigate when a crooked policeman is found murdered in a subway station. The case leads them to a doctor whose former girlfriends had all been infected with a virus normally found in animals - and an exhibitionist vet who might have helped the women contract it

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler's wife tells him about a teenage rape victim at the local hospital who is too frightened to go to the police. While trying to find out the girl's identity, he uncovers drug and alcohol abuse at the local school that has him fearing for his daughter's safety - and afraid he may jeopardise his spouse's job. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler investigate the rape of a cellist, and initially believe her conductor is to blame for the attack - especially given his penchant for erotic film-making. However, they soon discover an obsessive stalker is watching her from afar - and will stop at nothing to continue his monitoring. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The kidnapping of a baby at a grocery store leads the detectives to a shady racket that involves snatching infants and arranging to sell them for adoption. Unexpectedly, the investigation produces new leads on an old case involving a murdered mother and her missing child - which had been supervised by Cragen and his partner. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An HIV-positive man with a record of assaulting under-age girls is suspected of a series of rapes and murders - but Stabler's determination to charge him results in tragedy. Undeterred, the team pursues its new suspect, a rap promoter, though his cunning leaves little hope of a conviction. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: The Enemy Within
programme.desc: After a major Tal operative escapes CIA custody, Shepherd returns to Langley to help track the fugitive down. Shepherd and Keaton discover the CIA is withholding vital information

programme.title: The Enemy Within

Crime thriller based on the book by Mary Higgins Clark, starring Laura Leighton, Brandy Ledford and Gedeon Burkhard

programme.title: Mary Higgins Clark: He Sees You When You're Sleeping (2002)
programme.desc: A self-absorbed stockbroker is killed in an accident and discovers he is unable to enter Heaven until he changes his ways. To prove he has learnt his lesson, he is sent back to Earth, where his guardian angel informs him he must save an innocent family from gangsters. Fantasy crime drama, adapted from the novel by Mary Higgins Clark, starring Erika Eleniak, Cameron Bancroft, Greg Evigan and Pam Hyatt

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Mary Higgins Clark: He Sees You When You're Sleeping (2002)
programme.desc: A self-absorbed stockbroker is killed in an accident and discovers he is unable to enter Heaven until he changes his ways. Fantasy crime drama, adapted from the novel by Mary Higgins Clark, starring Erika Eleniak, Cameron Bancroft, Greg Evigan and Pam Hyatt

programme.title: Columbo: Old Fashioned Murder (1976)
programme.desc: A woman who has devoted her life to running her family's museum is outraged when her brother decides to sell it. Knowing of someone with criminal connections, she arranges to have her brother killed. However, just when she starts to think she has got away with it, a certain shabby sleuth is assigned to the case. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Joyce Van Patten, Celeste Holm and Tim O'Connor

programme.title: Columbo: A Stitch in Crime (1973)
programme.desc: Surgeon Barry Mayfield decides he needs get rid of his colleague to speed along a research project. When his partner requires heart surgery, Mayfield performs the operation himself and discreetly arranges some fatal complications. However, when he also murders a nurse who suspects the truth, he attracts the attention of the slovenly sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Leonard Nimoy and Will Geer

programme.title: Columbo: Murder Under Glass (1978)
programme.desc: The crumpled detective applies his well-honed instincts to a case of blackmail and murder in the world of haute cuisine when a restaurant owner is found dead after throwing a renowned food critic off his premises. Crime drama from The Silence of the Lambs director Jonathan Demme, starring Peter Falk, Louis Jourdan, Shera Danese and Richard Dysart

programme.title: Columbo: The Bye-Bye Sky-High IQ Murder Case (1977)
programme.desc: An accountant who has a reputation as being one of the brightest men in the world realises he has to kill a fellow genius to cover up his illegal activities. He puts his remarkable intelligence to the task of covering up every possible clue that could lead back to him - but has not counted on the equally sharp mind of slovenly sleuth Columbo. Crime drama, with Peter Falk and Theodore Bikel

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: As Stone takes on the case of a woman who killed her abusive husband, Benson and the team struggle with the idea of testifying against her

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Cabot takes a child abuse case, determined to find evidence that will convict the defendant. Her personal feelings tempt her to bend the rules, leading Stabler and Benson into an illegal search of the victim's home - a move that threatens all of their careers

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The stepdaughter of a judge known for his harsh sentencing of sex offenders is raped and murdered. Stabler and Benson's investigations reveal the rebellious girl had been involved with defendants in her stepfather's cases - and a revelation from her childhood leads the detectives to point the finger of suspicion at the victim's nearest and dearest. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A urologist is murdered, and his wife claims she was raped and robbed by the killer. Stabler and Benson suspect the doctor's mistress of being involved - until she too is sexually assaulted, and details of another relationship emerge, shedding light on the culprit's identity. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and another officer team up to track down a serial killer, focusing their investigation on a recently released sex offender - but fresh evidence leads them to believe he may have been wrongly convicted. Concerned that a miscarriage of justice could have occurred, they look over old case files and turn their attention to the suspect's neighbour, a man with employment links in the vicinity of seven murders around the country. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and David Keith

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When a man is shot outside a gay club, the detectives' investigations lead to his family, where they discover the wife of the dead man has a turbulent past. Forensic testing connects the victim to the filming of a porn movie - but the team does not expect to find out his spouse is also involved in the industry. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: The Enemy Within
programme.desc: When a highly sophisticated Tal operative evades FBI custody and flees to the Swiss embassy, the Cointel team must go under cover

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces across the country to reveal how CCTV and technological advances are helping to ensure convictions against shoplifters. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV chef Ainsley Harriott and presenter Anne Diamond take on the antiques-hunting challenge. Travelling in and around London on their search for auction-worthy items, Anne finds out why Britain became a nation of tea-lovers and Ainsley learns about the world's first celebrity chef

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping Feature. Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The story of the volunteer lifeboat crews and lifeguards of the RNLI who rescue thousands of people and save hundreds of lives around our coastline and on the River Thames every year. In the first episode, a father and daughter find themselves treading water for almost an hour hundreds of metres out at sea, and three fishermen swim for their lives as their trawler is smashed onto rocks in Ireland. Elsewhere in London, an unlucky tourist is minutes from hypothermia after falling into the Thames

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A kayaker goes missing for an hour off the coast of Bridlington, East Yorkshire, and a crew member on his first call-out races to a capsized dinghy to find a man losing consciousness in the water. Plus, when a fishing trawler sinks off the coast of the Shetland Islands, the fishermen and RNLI crew aboard must swim for their lives. Narrated by Steven Mackintosh

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Ireland, the Castletownbere crew race to rescue a lone yachtsman, who has lost his mast and is drifting 45 miles out to sea. Meanwhile, a team search for a woman in Minehead who has gone missing on a cliff top walk, and for an injured horse rider on a remote beach, the Porthcawl crew are the only way to emergency medical help

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Crews are tasked to find two teenage paddle boarders in Bangor, Northern Ireland, and the Tenby crew in west Wales must race to a dog who's fallen over a cliff. Meanwhile, on the north Norfolk coast, the harrowing hovercraft rescue of three young sisters still brings back painful memories for the Hunstanton crew

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In south west Wales, the Tenby lifeboat crew races to a kayaker with suspected back and neck injuries after being caught out by a freak wave. Neighbouring Cornish crews must join forces in an epic 11-hour battle to stop a 3,600-ton trading vessel breaking up on the rocks. And the Dart and Salcombe crews are astonished to be paged to the biggest shout of their lives

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On one of the hottest days of the year, the Dover RNLI crew race to a stricken Channel swimmer enduring difficulties within eyesight of the French coast. In Mudeford, there are growing fears for a dinghy sailor four miles out in rough seas and refusing to accept help. And the Tenby team are called to a runaway yacht which is on the verge of running aground

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: When a climber falls in a remote cave on the Gower Peninsula, the Mumbles crew are called in to search and rescue. In Portreath, lifeguards battle to save a teenager who's determined to surf despite red flag warnings. And on the Isle of Wight, the Yarmouth crew must locate a man in the water amongst over a thousand boats competing in the Round the Island Race

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Ilfracombe, a father and son are in danger after jumping into rescue a drowning dog. The Minehead crew must scour the cliffs and shoreline when a young woman goes missing in a distressed state. And when they're paged to find a man seen entering the Thames, the Tower team face the possibility they may not find him in time

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Aberystwyth crew must improvise when a local paraglider crash-lands at the bottom of steep cliffs. When an adventurous seal is discovered on the steps of a block of flats, Redcar's newest recruit is first on the scene, and in Minehead, a challenging rescue forces an inexperienced crew member to consider her future

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: As the summer sun comes out, so do the funseekers, and in Southend a family of five sightseeing a mile off shore are caught out by the tide. The Shoreham crew are paged in the early hours after a drunken festival-goer jumps in the river, and the Brighton crew fear the worst when a man tombstones off the pier at low tide

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Stockport, Greater Manchester, Sarah Moore sets about transforming a beaten-up chest of drawers, a sack barrow and some copper pipes. Thanks to the help of furniture designer Zoe and metal worker Bex Simon, Sarah later has money to hand over for all of her finds, but just how much cash was there to be made from the Stockport trash?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The experts head for Derbyshire, where Charles Hanson is on his home turf, enlisting the aid of his two-year-old daughter and hitching a ride on a horse and cart before investing in a collection of medieval items dredged from a river bed. Meanwhile, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the world's oldest lift shaft

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The experts head for Staffordshire, where Charles Hanson takes a look at a 1970s dress and Natasha Raskin Sharp thinks a model bulldog is likely to make a profit

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: The team transforms a picnic area into an operating theatre to save the victim of an explosion. A jockey is hurt after she is thrown by a horse

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A 12-hour day shift with West Midlands Ambulance Service paramedics sees them deal with problems and frustrations they experience when dealing with the growing number of patients undergoing mental health crises. A call comes in from a member of the public who has spotted a suicidal message on Twitter, while Dave and Lauren are called to an alcoholic patient who has overdosed

programme.title: Ambulance Australia
programme.desc: A Queensland ambulance crew respond to a life-threatening motorbike crash. Paramedics Ashley and Armahn treat a woman who has overdosed on drugs

programme.title: Ghost Adventures
programme.desc: Ghost hunters Zak Bagans, Nick Groff and Aaron Goodwin investigate spooky goings-on in South Carolina's Old Charleston Jail, which once accommodated the first female mass-murderer

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Part three of three. The team hear strange sounds in the Leopard Inn, and the poltergeist activity grows even more intense

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over the course of a year following life on five farms around Scotland. It is autumn on Lewis in the Outer Hebrides and sea shepherd Sandy gathers in a flock of wild sheep by boat, while on the mainland in the east Mel and Martin head to market to look for the perfect rams to impregnate their ewes. In central Scotland near Loch Lomond, hill farmers Bobby and Anne health-check their flock to make sure they are fit to breed, and in the far north John has 4,000 ewes to check, but is also expecting thousands of visitors from all over the world as he hosts the World Sheepdog Trials

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping Feature. Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Argyll, George and Sybil set off to gather in their sheep from over six square miles of rugged mountainside before taking some of them to market, while soon-to-be-wed couple Martin and Mel introduce their newly purchased rams to their flock of ewes. In the far north beyond Inverness, John heads to market to teach his 13-year-old son James how to buy a good flock of ewes, and Sandy sets off on a 1,600-mile journey from his home on Lewis to sell his specialist Hebridean mutton and beef around the UK

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, ex-barrister Sandy Granville and his fellow crofters collect their sheep from the moor and club together to dip them in insecticide before mating time. Meanwhile, on the mainland in the west, Sybil and George Macpherson gather their herd of cattle and separate the cows from their calves before they are sent off to lowland farms for the winter

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The early winter is a time of year that brings new life on the farms and Martin's sheds are full of pregnant cows - but there are two he is particularly concerned about. The vet arrives on Lewis to do an annual blood test on Sandy's Highland cattle during a Force 11 gale, while back on the mainland Sybil nearly gets carried away when she and George head to market to buy rams to breed with their ewes

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Christmas is looming, and in Inverness, Martin Irvine's sheds are now full of cows and their newborn calves. One was born prematurely and with such a low weigh his chances of survival don't look good. Cameras also follow the drama in Argyll where the weather takes a turn for the worse, just when Sybil and George Macpherson must load more than two tonnes of fleeces into a haulage lorry

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, crofter Sandy Granville assesses the damage to his croft after one of the worst storms on record. Meanwhile, farmers in the north of Aberdeen and near Inverness scan their pregnant ewes, and on the West Coast, two hill farmers try to return four male tup lambs to a close friend. Plus, John Scott and Martine Irvine head to the Stirling Bull Sales in the hope of selling their best bulls

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lambing season is just a month away for most of the farmers and to the east of Inverness Martin tends to 500 pregnant ewes and gets the birthing sheds ready with help from his dad Stevie. Further west in Argyll, George takes a group of Belgian hunters out on a deer-stalking trip, and later he and wife Sybil co-host a judging competition where knowledge of blackface sheep is put to the test. In the far north, John heads to the vet with a pedigree ewe that's pregnant with triplets

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and John hires extra help for the lambing season, with New Zealander Emma helping a ewe to give birth to triplets and hoping the smallest and weakest one will survive. Martin invests in some hi-tech kit to alert him when his cows are calving and collects semen from his prize bull to sell, while Mel spends her birthday at a cattle auction

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Central Scotland, Bobby and Anne must gather their pregnant ewes from the hills ready for lambing, while east of Inverness, Mel and Martin move into a caravan on site to deal with the imminent arrival of hundreds of lambs. On the Isle of Lewis, Sandy makes tweed curtains for the holiday let as Easter approaches, and an unexpected tragedy hits the Lennoxes

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Sybil prepares for George's 50th birthday and the arrival of her sister and niece from England, while persistent rain means Martin's cattle are yet to be turned out of their winter sheds into the fields. Bobby and Anne hold an open day to educate the public about farming and John has to urgently call the vet when one of his heifers gets into difficulty calving

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Summer arrives, and in the east of Scotland, Martin prepares two young bulls for the biggest event of the farming calendar - the Royal Highland Show in Edinburgh. First they need their nose rings, an accessory no bull can do without. Martin and Mel's wedding is also looming, and while he goes for his final kilt fitting, Mel meets her Dad to discuss their plans

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms and then sells a chair, an art deco toy and a broken table about to be dumped, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: In Liverpool, Natasha Raskin Sharp hopes for a big hit with a Sgt Pepper-style military jacket, while Charles Hanson finds two maritime-themed stained glass panels

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Helicopter ER

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Specialist paramedic Peter is working solo in a rapid-response vehicle during a busy night shift, and as almost all the ambulances are assigned, he is kept busy dealing with the backlog. His patients include a 16-year-old who has been attacked with a machete and a man who has been found in a canal. Then when he is dealing with a low-priority patient, a call comes in about a double shooting, and two air ambulances are scrambled to the location where it is unknown whether the gunman is still present

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Part two of two. Newly crowned Queen Morgana begins her evil reign over the innocent citizens of Camelot while Uther can only stand by and watch as Morgause's immortal army causes the kingdom to crumble. On the outskirts of the city, Arthur regains his strength and decides to mount a counter-attack, but Merlin fears he will not be able to stop the deathless troops and tries to reclaim the great sword Excalibur to stop the destruction of all he holds dear

programme.title: Bee Gees: One Night Only
programme.desc: Barry, Robin and Maurice Gibb perform their greatest hits, such as Jive Talkin', How Deep Is Your Love and Stayin' Alive, in a concert at the MGM Grand, Las Vegas, in 1997. They are joined by Celine Dion on the track Immortality, and also sing some of the music they wrote but which was made popular by other artists, including Islands in the Stream, Guilty and Heartbreaker

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An insight into the case of Dennis Nilsen, the London-based killer who murdered at least 12 men, and was caught when he started trying to flush his victims' remains down the toilet

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: The presenter explores the case of John Straffen, who killed two young girls in the summer of 1951. He was found to be unfit to plead and committed to Broadmoor Hospital, but during a brief escape from the facility in 1952 he killed again. It was determined that the cause of his behaviour was severe brain damage, traced back to an attack of encephalitis as a child in India. When he died in 2007, he was the longest-serving prisoner in Britain

programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
programme.desc: An accident involving a small dose of radiation transports O'Brien five hours into the future where he witnesses his own death in a brawl between Klingons and Romulans. programme.title: Ghost Whisperer
programme.desc: In the 100th episode of the paranormal drama, Melinda is contacted by a ghost with news that a bomb has gone missing from a hidden munitions dump. The spirit warns her that the thief intends to use the device to kill someone, and urges her to locate it before disaster strikes. Directed by and starring Jennifer Love Hewitt

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Evil sorceress Morgana summons mighty gatekeeper the Cailleach, who opens a door to another world from which hellish creatures emerge. As Arthur and his loyal knights take up arms, Merlin discovers the only thing that will stop them is an unimaginable sacrifice. Colin Morgan returns as the young wizard in the first of a two-part story, guest starring Gemma Jones (the Harry Potter movies) and Emilia Fox (Silent Witness)

programme.title: Micky Flanagan: Back in the Game
programme.desc: An evening of comedy, recorded as part of the funnyman's 2013 sell-out Back in the Game tour, which saw Micky play to more than 500,000 people across the UK and Ireland. Performing here to his home crowd at London's Hackney Empire, the stand-up star regales his audience with tales of middle age, marriage and a man's prerogative

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: A drink-driver quickly sobers up when he makes an ill-advised attempt to hide from dog handler Steve, German shepherd Razor and a police helicopter. Exeter Road Crime Unit officers Mike and Chris arrest a young man wanted for burglary, and the Ashburton Tactical Aid Group launches a major drug operation. Plus, footage from America featuring a joy-riding grandmother

programme.title: The Force: North East
programme.desc: The dog unit deals with a man who has barricaded himself in his house with his Rottweiler, while a woman thought to have overdosed disappears

programme.title: The Force: North East
programme.desc: A trail of blood leaves officers puzzled, a man high on drink and drugs launches a tirade of abuse and a domestic fight leads to an arrest

programme.title: Border Patrol
programme.desc: Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders.

programme.desc: Fly-on-the-wall documentary accessing the world of national security at Australia's biggest airports as customs and immigration officers tackle drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A devious jewellery snatcher is tracked by police, and a thief in a kitchen is caught on camera. Dominic Littlewood presents

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood presents more examples of filmed evidence that have helped to bring criminals to book, including animals put in danger when rubbish is dumped and an armed robber at large in the North West of England

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood looks at filmed evidence used by the police, businesses and the public to catch criminals. Featuring a man on a train who steals bags from above victims' heads and armed men in white chemical suits as they steal jewellery worth a quarter of a million pounds from a family business

programme.title: 999 Frontline
programme.desc: Crime scene investigators collect forensic evidence in Luton, while in Chelmsford, paramedics attend to a pregnant woman with severe abdominal pains, before taking her to hospital for further treatment

programme.title: 999 Frontline
programme.desc: Riot squad officers and firefighters in Southend are called to an incident in which a man is threatening to set a building alight. Plus, police in Ipswich evict a group of trouble-makers from a football ground and prepare security for the final game of the season

programme.title: 999 Frontline
programme.desc: The East Anglian Air Ambulance crew take off to help casualties in rural Norfolk, while Essex traffic police are called to the scene of a road accident in Harlow

programme.title: 999 Frontline
programme.desc: Ipswich police arrest three men on suspicion of trafficking crack cocaine, while officers from Southend fire station's Red Watch race to free a motorist trapped in the wreckage of his badly damaged car

programme.title: 999 Frontline
programme.desc: Bedfordshire traffic police race to the scene of a fatal joyriding incident, while A&E consultant Keith Walters employs his manipulation skills to put a dislocated shoulder back in its socket

programme.title: 999 Frontline
programme.desc: Norfolk firefighters don breathing apparatus to tackle a blazing bungalow. Crime scene investigators are also kept busy, meticulously collecting DNA evidence, fingerprints and photographs to aid their attempts to catch ram-raiders, motorbike thieves and vandals

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: PC Dave Smith and his Belgian shepherd Kelly race to the scene of a burglary at a pet-food store, but when the suspects are found to have fled the scene, police helicopter India 99 is used to help track them down. PC Adele Gibson and her sidekick Diesel take part in a dawn drugs raid, and the London heats of the annual police dog trials give PC Andy Miller's dog Nelson the chance to test his skills. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Sgt Midgley tries to qualify as a dog handler after 24 years on the force but 14-month-old springer spaniel Ruby puts both their futures in jeopardy. The Greater Manchester Police dog squad remembers a close call with football fans during the 2008 Uefa Cup Final, and the 55-strong team deals with a siege involving a man with a knife, who is threatening to kill his family. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: London's Metropolitan Police dog unit tests the skills of its leading specialist search dogs, pitting spaniels Diesel and Billy against each other as they hunt for hidden heroin. On the streets of north London, shift leader Pete Madden puts his Alsatian Brodie in the line of fire when they target two armed robbers, but as the trail runs cold they find themselves in pursuit of a gang of car vandals. Ken Stott narrates

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: PC Greaves and his four-year-old German shepherd Riley track down a motorist who panicked when he saw flashing blue lights and ran off, while PC Whitaker and his dog Roscoe have to disarm a man who has a 12-inch knife. During a search of three vehicles used by a suspected drug dealer, sniffer spaniel Smut and handler PC Carlin find a wallet with almost £1,000 inside it hidden under a dashboard. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Father-and-son dog handlers John and Kevin Tozer join forces with their Alsatians Charlie and Floyd as the Metropolitan Police dog unit raids a suspected cannabis den. However, retirement beckons for John's canine companion, leaving 13-month-old Herbie as a possible replacement - if he can make it through basic training to join London's dog squad elite ranks. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Billy the new sniffer spaniel finds live ammunition during a search for drugs, and a stabbing on the streets of London brings together two of the city's leading Alsatian dog teams. PC Mandy Chapman and her canine companion Karly are sent in to enforce a court warrant against a dangerous dog, while Sergeant Peter Madden and Brodie are on the trail of burglars using stolen cars as getaway vehicles. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Monkeys: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Biologist Patrick Aryee travels to Central and South America, Africa and Asia to reveal the stories behind the origins of each of the world's primate species, coming face-to-face with some of the planet's most charismatic and mischievous monkeys

programme.title: Monkeys: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Biologist Patrick Aryee takes a closer look at the rise of the monkey, exploring the many diverse species and their place in primate evolution

programme.title: Monkeys: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Biologist Patrick Aryee examines the development of monkeys from Central and South America and their evolutionary journey dating back more than 40 million years

programme.title: Ballet Shoes

programme.title: Anoop and The Elephant (1972)
programme.desc: Two English children meet an Indian boy and his pet elephant, and help to keep the animal out of the hands of a greedy circus owner. Family drama, with Anoop Singh and Phil Daniels

programme.title: London Districts

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Zoe's divorce comes through, and while Alec is keen to celebrate, she is not so sure

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Lee, Bermondsey and Stratford. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sisters Sharon and Tracey's lives are changed for ever when their husbands leave for work and never return. First episode of the popular comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The sisters face up to the realities of life with their husbands in prison, and try to explain things to Tracey's son, Garth. Classic comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Spring and Port Wine (1970)
programme.desc: A tyrannically strict Lancashire father brings about the downfall of his family by forcing his daughter to eat a meal against her will. Drama, starring James Mason, Diana Coupland, Susan George and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: In the Doghouse (1961)
programme.desc: A newly qualified vet struggles to compete against an unscrupulous rival. Comedy, starring Leslie Phillips, Peggy Cummins, Hattie Jacques and James Booth

programme.title: Moby Dick (1956)
programme.desc: Obsessive skipper Captain Ahab leads his ship and crew on an epic quest to find and kill the legendary white whale that bit off his leg - but his lust for vengeance endangers all their lives. John Huston's seafaring adventure, based on Herman Melville's novel, starring Gregory Peck, Richard Baseheart, Orson Welles and Leo Genn

programme.title: Our Queen
programme.desc: Feature-length documentary filmed throughout 2012, providing an in-depth portrait of Elizabeth II during her Diamond Jubilee year. Prince Charles and other members of the royal family share their stories, providing insights into the Queen's life over an important and busy 12 months filled with celebrations. The programme also reveals what really happens when the prime minister sits down with the monarch, and the lid is lifted on that memorable 007 moment at the London Olympic Games opening ceremony

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef puts a vegetarian twist on a classic dish as she indulges in comfort food

programme.title: A London Community in Lockdown
programme.desc: A heartfelt look at life during lockdown in the Listraban community in London, a record of a moment in time that will be in the history books for generations to come

programme.title: Music Within (2007)
programme.desc: The true story of Richard Pimentel, a brilliant public speaker with a troubled past, who returns from Vietnam severely hearing-impaired and finds a new purpose in his landmark efforts on the behalf of Americans with disabilities. Biographical drama, starring Ron Livingston, Melissa George and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson

programme.title: The Hitman's Apprentice (2012)
programme.desc: A bumbling teenager is offered a job by his mother's crime boss boyfriend. He is given the task of driving one of the gangster's business associates around town, only to find himself an assistant to a hitman on his last assignment, a mission that leads to a deadly encounter with a mysterious woman. Thriller, starring Tim Roth, Jack O'Connell and Talulah Riley

programme.title: Helga, She Wolf of Stilberg (1978)
programme.desc: A sadistic female Nazi officer runs a women's prison during the Second World War, and forces the inmates to be her sexual playthings. However, the latest arrival, a determined resistance fighter, proves to be far more difficult to dominate. Prison drama, starring Malisa Longo and Patrizia Gori. In French

programme.desc: Andrew White treks up The Great Orme in North Wales and beside a railway in West Yorkshire
