programme.desc: A series of animated fairy tales read by various celebrities

programme.title: The Scarlet Pimpernel
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. During the French Revolution a daring Englishman known as the Scarlet Pimpernel rescues noble families from the guillotine.

programme.title: Operation Third Form (1966)
programme.desc: A trio of youngsters tries to stop a gang of crooks and save an old junk collector from being accused of theft. Children's drama, starring Kevin Bennett and Sydney Bromley

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec gets on so well with Zoe's parents that she begins to feel left out

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec agrees to give Zoe driving lessons, but it soon puts pressure on their relationship

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby

programme.title: The Best of Men
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the birth of the Paralympics in 1948. Pioneering neurologist Dr Ludwig Guttmann arrives at Stoke Mandeville Hospital in Buckinghamshire and begins to transform the lives of his patients - Second World War servicemen with spinal cord injuries. He suggests using sport as a key part of their rehabilitation, and gets them involved in activities including wheelchair polo and basketball. Starring Eddie Marsan, Rob Brydon, George Mackay and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Doctor at Large (1957)
programme.desc: Newly qualified doctor Simon Sparrow leaves St Swithin's Hospital and relocates to the countryside in search of a job at two prospering practices. However, before long he runs into fearsome former mentor Sir Lancelot Spratt. Comedy, starring Dirk Bogarde, James Robertson Justice and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Our Queen
programme.desc: Feature-length documentary filmed throughout 2012, providing an in-depth portrait of Elizabeth II during her Diamond Jubilee year. Prince Charles and other members of the royal family share their stories, providing insights into the Queen's life over an important and busy 12 months filled with celebrations. The programme also reveals what really happens when the prime minister sits down with the monarch, and the lid is lifted on that memorable 007 moment at the London Olympic Games opening ceremony

programme.title: The Last Days of Anne Boleyn
programme.desc: On Friday, May 19, 1536, Henry VIII's second wife Anne Boleyn became the first British queen to be executed, but the circumstances of her death remain shrouded in mystery and contradiction. Hilary Mantel, Philippa Gregory and David Starkey are among the leading authors and historians sifting through the evidence to determine why Henry had her killed

programme.title: The Crouches
programme.desc: Natalie plots revenge on Roly after he forces Grandma out into the cold, while Grandpa dreams of his own bachelor pad and Aiden struggles with his work experience placement. Sitcom, starring Robbie Gee, Jo Martin, Mona Hammond and Rudolph Walker

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Pretentious Alan, violent Kix and sex-mad Eleanor are part of a 1960s group of poets, artists and angry young men who convene for a weekend of amusement at a large villa overlooking the sea. Comedy, starring Peter Richardson, Adrian Edmondson, Dawn French, Jennifer Saunders and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Away (2016)
programme.desc: In Blackpool, an unlikely friendship blossoms between a lonely, suicidal widower and a young woman trying to escape an abusive ex-boyfriend. Drama, starring Timothy Spall, Juno Temple and Matt Ryan

programme.title: Brimstone and Treacle (1982)
programme.desc: Asinister stranger worms his way into a middle-aged couple's home by offering to take care of their comatose daughter - but it is only once he's part of the family that his true nature shines through. Drama based on Dennis Potter's TV play, starring Sting, Denholm Elliott, Joan Plowright and Suzanna Hamilton

programme.title: This That and the Other (1970)
programme.desc: Anthology of three comic tales. A fading movie star tries to seduce a producer, a suicidal loner is snapped out of his misery by a hippy, and a taxi driver suffers a blow to the head that leaves him experiencing erotic hallucinations. Comedy, starring Victor Spinetti, Dennis Waterman, Vanda Hudson and John Bird

programme.title: The Crouches
programme.desc: Natalie plots revenge on Roly after he forces Grandma out into the cold, while Grandpa dreams of his own bachelor pad and Aiden struggles with his work experience placement. Sitcom, starring Robbie Gee, Jo Martin, Mona Hammond and Rudolph Walker

programme.title: Pop Pirates (1984)
programme.desc: A pop group enters a talent contest, but their chances are jeopardised by the lead singer's involvement in video piracy. Drama, starring Spencer Chandler and Roger Daltrey

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The vikings visit a village that is supposedly being terrorised by a monster - and Vic meets a like-minded youngster who is determined to prove that the creature is not real

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Vic comes to the rescue of a princess, who is heading to a storytelling festival

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band makes a stand against global warming by broadcasting a benefit concert to the world from a science station in the Arctic

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: A woman who is Kate's exact double goes missing in the Himalayas just before her engagement party, so Kate has to take her place while Kylie and Kim search for her

programme.title: The Canterville Ghost
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. The story of a ghost who is faced with a new set of inhabitants who refuse to be scared by him

programme.title: Rockets in the Dunes (1960)
programme.desc: A Devon beach is used by local children for racing sand yachts, but their fun may be at an end when the army plan to use the dunes as a training ground. The youngsters rally the local community to save their beach, but the discovery of disused mines complicates things further. Family drama, starring Richard Adam and Fiona Davie

programme.desc: Zoe is not seeing as much of Alec as she would like, so she suggests that he move in with her

programme.title: May to December

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing Crystal Palace and Shoreditch. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien may have ulterior motives for inviting Tracey to a posh event, while Sharon makes a tidy profit out of fake Rolling Stones T-shirts. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Far from the Madding Crowd (1967)
programme.desc: A beautiful woman arouses the ardour of three rival suitors in 19th-century rural Dorset and, having flirted and toyed with their affections, she chooses to marry a caddish, gambling soldier - a decision that leads to a sequence of harrowing events. John Schlesinger's adaptation of Thomas Hardy's classic novel, with Julie Christie, Alan Bates, Terence Stamp and Peter Finch

programme.title: A Matter of Choice (1963)
programme.desc: A married woman finds herself caught in a moral dilemma when children inadvertently cause the death of her diabetic lover. Drama, starring Anthony Steel, Jeanne Moody and Ballard Berkeley

programme.title: This Is London
programme.desc: Take a tour of London through some of the city's most exciting, trendiest businesses and products, featuring ads from Elan Cafe, Cityspace, The Oblique Life and more

programme.title: LNYDP - A Turn of Year Tradition
programme.desc: Philip Glenister takes a behind the scenes look at London's New Year's Day Parade. Visiting iconic locations associated with the street spectacular and sees how the parade prepares the night before

programme.title: Diana: Last Days of a Princess
programme.desc: Genevieve O'Reilly and Patrick Baladi star as Diana, Princess of Wales and Dodi Al Fayed in a docu-drama charting the couple's last days, culminating in the fatal crash in the Alma Tunnel, Paris. Including interviews with Mohamed Al Fayed, bodyguard Kez Wingfield and masseuse Myriah Daniels

programme.title: The Food Hospital
programme.desc: A 47-year-old man receives a shock when consultant surgeon Shaw Somers reveals he is suffering from metabolic syndrome and his body is more like that of a 60-year-old. A 23-year-old woman seeks help with her extremely bad breath, GP Giovanni Miletto puts together an experimental plan to combat chronic fatigue syndrome, and Pixie McKenna discovers how diet can influence people's chances of developing cancer

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Series of one-off comedy dramas

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Best of The Tube

programme.title: Strippers vs Werewolves (2012)
programme.desc: Lapdancer Justice kills a customer when he undergoes a terrifying transformation, and the body is quietly disposed of. The dead man was a werewolf, and at the next full moon, the other members of his pack plan to seek revenge. Justice and her fellow strippers have one month to prepare themselves for a battle to the death. Comedy horror, starring Adele Silva and Robert Englund

programme.title: The Hitman's Apprentice (2012)
programme.desc: A bumbling teenager is offered a job by his mother's crime boss boyfriend. He is given the task of driving one of the gangster's business associates around town, only to find himself an assistant to a hitman on his last assignment, a mission that leads to a deadly encounter with a mysterious woman. Thriller, starring Tim Roth, Jack O'Connell and Talulah Riley

programme.title: Felicity (1978)
programme.desc: A teenager in a remote Catholic girls' school seeks solace from her repressive life in reading erotic novels and having a secret love affair with one of her classmates. programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Former Dancing on Ice judges Jason Gardiner and Ashley Roberts, Birds of a Feather actress Linda Robson and actor and TV presenter Christopher Biggins compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. programme.desc: Phil Taylor v Raymond van Barneveld. Action from the semi-final in the 2013 World Championship at Alexandra Palace. programme.desc: Carl Froch v George Groves. Another chance to see the bout for the WBA and IBF Super Middleweight titles, held at the Phones 4u Arena in Manchester. Froch avenged one of only two career defeats when outpointing Mikkel Kessler in his previous fight, and his greater experience at the highest level meant he went into this contest as the favourite. However, he could not afford to take Groves lightly, as the man 11 years his junior entered the match with an unblemished professional record
